episodes.title: The Young Americans at Sathya Sai Baba's Ashram in 1970
episodes.description:
The Young Americans at Sathya Sai Baba's Ashram in 1970.

00:00 Intro
00:08 Mandir
00:24 Baba on the Balcony
00:37 Baba in his Dinning Room
00:55 Michele outside the Accommodation
01:16 Michele recounts the early days
02:44 Puttaparthi
03:03 Dr Jaya Lakshmi’s Rooftop
03:29 The streets of Puttaparthi
03:42 Five woman and a baby
04:00 Kasturi
04:20 Dussehra
04:48 Guru Poornima in Prasanthi Nilayam, 1970
07:40 Group photo, 1970
08:17 VJ and India
08:30 On the road to Bangalore 
10:29 Brindavan rooftop
11:01 The Pump House
11:24 The Well
11:39 Group photo after an interview with Sathya Sai Baba
12:08 Brindavan Darshan 
12:38 On Baba’s verandah in Brindavan
13:06 Brindavan Darshan 
14:12 Resting under a tree in Brindavan
14:25 A Tonga to Puttaparthi
14:52 Coolie crossing the Chitravathi to Puttaparthi
15:00 Puttaparthi
15:06 Bruce Bradbury arrives in April, 1970. He recalls this time in 1990
16:14 Mandir
16:39 Construction
19:25 Dussehra, Prasanthi Nilayam, 1972
22:10 Cathy Bayer receiving packets of vibhuti from Sathya Sai Baba
22:20 Cathy Bayer receiving a sari from Sathya Sai Baba
22:32 Painting the signs inside the Poornachandra
22:51 Christmas 1971, Madras
23:25 Delhi, 1972
23:43 In Anantapur, Gale Molloy is seen here with Sathya Sai Baba
24:03 Janet Bock, Anantapur
24:08 Sathya Sai Baba and Indra Devi at Anantapur, early 1970s
24:41 Indra Devi, Janet Bock and Diana Riordan with Sathya Sai Baba in the Brindavan Ashram, 1972
24:48 Baba Muktananda and Ram Dass visit Sathya Sai Baba in 1971
25:28 Following his restoration of life, Walter and Elsie Cowan are remarried in 1972, by Sathya Sai Baba in the Brindavan Ashram, Whitefield
28:00 The two sisters in Varanasi
28:08 A Taxi Ride in India
37:29 Other Photos

Sathya Sai Baba PLAYLIST https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLfvx-cbEOmxDfWCd0B3gVwxgPF4O7qvEy

Interviews with Sathya Sai Baba https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLfvx-cbEOmxBOphkiR_QPIHgbyR_Xs9Jz

Ashrams of India Volumes 1 & 2 : Prasanthi Nilayam, in Puttaparthi, Andhra Pradesh (125 km NNE of Bangalore) is Sathya Sai Baba's main ashram. Brindavan, in Whitefield (on the outskirts of Bangalore) is the second ashram. Sai Shruti in Kodaikanal, a hill station in the south of India, is the third ashram.

Ashrams of India - ☆☆☆☆☆ - 5 out of 5 stars "Masterful - a well researched, serious look at many famous ashrams. Masterfully written and produced. So much detail and care has gone into this project which is a labour of love. We may only bow!"

Ashrams of India https://ashramsofindia.com/ "A massive 1749 page two volume book set that explores over 500 ashrams, temples, and other significant sites of eastern religious and spiritual interest located throughout the Indian sub-continent. The compilers have backed up their explorations with reems of classical, historical, and recent background information." - The Wanderling https://the-wanderling.com/

Ashrams of India is a travel guide that explores over 500 places of spiritual interest for the modern traveller.

Westerners had been visiting the ashrams of saints since the early part of the twentieth century. Their stories and the places that they visited, many of which are still in existence, are gathered together here in 'Ashrams of India' with practical advice including contact details, directions, and coordinates that link to Google Maps.

episodes.title: Cult Explosion (1980)
episodes.description:
This is an excerpt from Cult Explosion (1980) https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=F__iFrxB0Fs&t=1486s

Cult Explosion looks at religions groups considered by some to be cults include Hare Krishna, the Manson Family, the People's Temple, Scientology, Christian Science, Unity, Worldwide Church of God, Jehovah Witness, the Latter-Day Saints, the Unification Church, and Transcendental Meditation, presenting the expertise of Dr. Walter Martin, Eldridge Cleaver, and Brooks Alexander.

Based on the book by David Hunt, this is a Jeremiah Films release, distributed by New Liberty Enterprises.
subjects: The Church Of Jesus Christ Of Latter-Day Saints

episodes.title: SAI BABA MAHIMA (1990) English Language Version
episodes.description:
In 1990, several Italian-language documentary films were produced, specifically focusing on the Lila (divine play), Mahima (miracles/glory), and Upadesha (teachings) of Sathya Sai Baba. 

These documentaries were created by the Sri Sathya Sai Global Council/Sai Organization to introduce his message to Italian speakers. 

These films, along with translated lectures, were part of an effort to introduce the concepts of Lila, Mahima, and Upadesha to a wider audience, which Sathya Sai Baba described as the three phases of his mission. 

• SAI BABA LILA IL GIOCO DIVINO This documentary centers on the "divine play" or early life of Sathya Sai Baba (the first 16 years of his life), presenting him as the divine incarnation who came to restore Dharma. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hFOcwFhOl2o

ENGLISH Language version https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nmGch5eOBEs

• SAI BABA MAHIMA This film covers the "miracles" or "divine glory" (Mahima) and extraordinary powers of Sathya Sai Baba, exploring his ability to perform acts that defy natural laws. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=5FcyMfH4umo

ENGLISH Language version https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dQifrjOtIMo

• SAI BABA UPADESHA This documentary focuses on the "teachings" or "spiritual instructions" (Upadesha) given by Sathya Sai Baba, showcasing his role as a spiritual guru. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=scq1OcZklC4

ENGLISH Language version TBA

Related 1990s Italian Documentaries:

• Who's Sai Baba? (1990) An Italian documentary film directed by Victor J. Tognola. It features interviews with N. Kasturi and other devotees. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2FhqPZb-3V8

• A Trip to India (Un Viaggio in India - 1993): A documentary by Giuseppe Secca in Italian about his journey to visit Sathya Sai Baba https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=8ycJ1XYYNpI

ENGLISH Language version https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=I889uWJ-5xk

episodes.title: Sree Chakravarti and Shirdi Sai Baba
episodes.description:
Sree Chakravarti and Shirdi Sai Baba is from https://www.youtube.com/@ThinkingAllowedTV

"This treatment is no hushed, mystical, spiritual experience," writes Veronica Hauge, "For a moment, before she starts to treat you, Sreema will sit quietly, her face turned away as she takes a deep breath to center herself and concentrate her powers. 

But while her hand vibrates, she will chat about everything under the sun, smiling and laughing." Hauge is relating her experience with Sree Chakravarti, a self-described "Delhi housewife" who possesses an extraordinary power to diagnose and treat illnesses. 

"My theory," explains Sreema, "is that sickness is caused when electrons in the atoms of the ailing area act abnormally. It seems my right hand possesses some kind of magnetic vibration, and I believe this vibration, or radiation, helps to adjust the electrons and create a magnetic field that balances the body. I believe that my power to heal comes from Sai Baba, the saint of Shirdi (who passed on in 1918)."

Sreema's book, A Healer's Journey, is a kind of shaman's Travels of Marco Polo, with our heroine periodically departing her relatively sheltered life in Delhi to heal the ill and convert the skeptical. She travels far and wide, treating the Saudi royal family, dumbfounding groups of Western doctors in Lebanon, the United Kingdom and America, and in general demonstrating a truly extraordinary gift. The book abounds with descriptions and testimonies of her healings. 

James George, former ambassador of Canada to India (also Sri Lanka and Nepal), testifies that Sreema healed a tumor on his back. George told Hinduism Today, "She is one of those exceptionally rare people who know the inner power is not their own, but is a power they serve. She is an exceptional soul."

Ashrams of India Volume 1 : Samadhi Mandir of Shirdi Sai Baba

Shirdi Sai Baba (27 or 28 September 1838 - 15 October 1918). Also known as Sai Baba of Shirdi and Shirdi Baba, he was regarded by his devotees as a saint, fakir, and satguru. He was revered by both his Hindu and Muslim devotees. During and as well as after his life, it remained uncertain if he was a Hindu or a Muslim, this however was of no consequence to Sai Baba himself. Sai Baba opposed all persecution based on religion or caste. Although Sai Baba himself led the life of an ascetic, he advised his followers to lead an ordinary family life, encouraging them to pray, chant God’s name, and read holy scriptures. He advised his devotees to lead a moral life, help others, love every living being without any discrimination, and develop two important features of character: devotion to the Guru (Sraddha) and waiting cheerfully with patience and love.

Shirdi Sai Baba https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=eGHWlPBrh44

episodes.title: Sathya Sai Baba 50th Birthday Celebrations
episodes.description:
This 1976 Documentary is of the 50th birthday celebrations (23rd November, 1975) of Sathya Sai Baba that was a 10 day (14th - 24th November) Brahmotsavam (a festival of Divine bliss and ecstasy).

In this film, we see the first Rathotsavam and the inauguration of the Sarva Dharma Aikya Stupa, interviews by devotees such as Dr. S Bhagavantham and Dr. John Hislop. 

Sathya Sai Baba arrives in a helicopter on the morning of His birthday, officiating the 3rd World Conference of SSSIO, presiding over the Purushottama Yagna, inaugurating the Sarva Dharma Stupa, and swinging on the Jhoola on the final evening.

This film has been previously posted on the channel as Sathya Sai Baba (November, 1975).

The film with description https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=X9zmBTFvUfA

episodes.title: Richard Bock Films : 1980 Sathya Sai Baba 55th Birthday
episodes.description:
Richard Bock Films : 1980 Sathya Sai Baba 55th Birthday

Richard Bock Films https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLfvx-cbEOmxCJUpc0d5SuUSwLf8JprVXu

Richard Bock joined Indra Devi in India for Baba’s forty-third birthday in November 1968, and returned with eight millimetre film and the recordings of Baba which became the LP entitled, Sathya Sai Baba Chants the Bhajans.

Richard Bock was the Founder-President of Pacific Jazz and World Pacific Records. Through a long personal and professional relationship with Pandit Ravi Shankar, the world renowned sitar maestro, Mr. Bock was instrumental in introducing classical Indian music to large audiences in the West. He has produced several films on Sathya Sai Baba.

episodes.title: Howard Levin's interviews with Sathya Sai Baba
episodes.description:
American Howard Levin first arrives on the Whitefield ashram Brindavan, June 5th, 1970. He visited India during the late 1960s and while on his travels, he had heard about Sathya Sai Baba from others who had visited his ashram in South India. Howard decided to visit. He enjoyed many interviews with Sai Baba, and also experienced other phenomena that took place around the ashram. His book, Good Chances is an account from those days. Toward the end Sai Baba said to him “You must remember these days we’ve spent together. Now it is outside, next memory goes into your mind, then a permanent picture is there. When you all go back to America, if you think of these ‘good chances’ with Swami, it will be the same as meditation.

Howard’s books ~ 'Good Chances' (1985) and the sequel 'Heart to Heart' (1996).

00:00 Intro
00:14 Howard speaks at the 5th Annual Southwest Regional Conference of the US Sai Organisation, September 2nd, 1990, Dallas, Texas. This is an edit excerpt from https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=8YQtU1vhooA

episodes.title: God's Pharmacy of Herbs ~ Maria Treben (1984)
episodes.description:
Maria Treben became famous through her two books: Health Through God's Pharmacy and Maria Treben's Cures. The first was translated into 24 languages and sold over 8 million copies.

Treben addressed seminars and presented at natural health conferences in Germany, Austria and across Europe, attracting hundreds of people. She is perceived as a pioneer of the renewed interest for natural remedies and traditional medicine at the end of the 20th century.

Remedies: Treben used traditional German and Eastern European remedies handed down from previous generations. She only used local herbs and always accompanied her remedies with advice on diet. She commonly used Thyme, Greater Celandine, Ramsons, Speedwell, Calamus, Chamomile, Nettle and Lady's Mantle. She treated a broad range of conditions from psoriasis to constipation and diabetes to insomnia. She used her own recipes as well as traditional healing remedies like Swedish bitters that she used as a cure-all. Some of her remedies and advice proved to be controversial, such as some of the more esoteric ingredients used in the greater Swedish bitters recipe. To this day she is widely read and referred to for her knowledge of European medicinal herbs.

episodes.title: Whicker's World India ~ Shree Rajneesh Ashram (1978)
episodes.description:
Alan Whicker, Consistently Dapper ! Filmed in June, 1978.

People have said that I am too non-committal. I don’t think it is my job to pontificate. For example, take this series on India. I don’t think it is my job to say to the viewer “This is India, and it is absolutely marvellous, everything they do is wonderful.”

I also don’t think it is my job to say: “This is India, they have unbelievable squalor, incredible corruption, so much endemic sickness.” I don’t think either of these roles is right. I think it is my job to say: “This is India, like everything else there is light and shade.” If you watch the programmes carefully you will see what I think, but I will never go out of my way to say that Mrs. Ghandi is iniquitous or alternatively Mrs. Ghandi is wonderful.

I cannot find a cause 15 times a year. There is a certain type of television journalist who goes around the world looking for spastic children. “Bring out your unemployed, your distressed, what about the tea planters who are paid only 2p a day! Bring out your spastic babies, your blind, your paraplegic and we will do a soul-searing report on their distress.” It is very easy to do but I think that is irresponsible. I don’t subscribe to it. I think one has to be more responsible. One is not looking for sensation, what one is looking for is interest and pleasure and delight and information.

In my first programme in the Indian series I did two long interviews with two people who were so enchanting that I have seen this programme about 500 times and I love every minute of it. Indians are remarkably inhibited about so many things. They over-compensate for an inferiority complex. They have a superiority complex, they have hang-ups, they have a love-hate relationship with England and they don’t talk easily at all.

Rajneesh PLAYLIST https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLfvx-cbEOmxDpCgPt7o_4TYRkmB1OcSRb

1 Whicker's World : India : He's a Stuck Up Maharajah - I'm Not Going to Marry Him - Tue, Oct 10, 1978. Alan Whicker visits Rajasthan to examine Indian royal marriages. He meets the Maharajah of Jodphur and his wife, and rides their royal train. Whicker also meets the Princess of Alwar, a widow who is forbidden to remarry.

2 Whicker's World : India : Elephants Go Into All the Gardens - They're Just Like Rabbits - Tue, Oct 17, 1978. Alan Whicker visits Kerala to see the martial art of Kalarippayat, celebrates Passover with an old Jewish community, attends a festival where elephants in gold armour engage in a battle of Umbrellas, and meets some Britons who live there.

3 Whicker's World : India : A Form of Frightener Put on by the Union - Tue, Oct 24, 1978
Alan Whicker visits the High Ranges of North Travancore in India and looks at the lives of the few remaining British tea planters.

4 Whicker's World : India : Even Though You're a Maharajah, You're a Human-Being First - Tue, Oct 31, 1978. Alan Whicker visits several of the Maharajahs of Rajasthan and watches a polo match played on bicycles. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=lhk5-Cw7dUY

5 Whicker's World : India : Don't Feel Guilty About Your Cadillac - And I Won't Feel Guilty About My Bicycle: Part 1 - Tue, Nov 7, 1978. In the first of a two-part edition, Alan Whicker visits the city of Bombay, meeting some of its wealthiest residents, including film stars and industrialists and inhabitants of the shanty slum areas.

6 Whicker's World : India : Don't Feel Guilty About Your Cadillac - And I Won't Feel Guilty About My Bicycle: Part 2. Tue, Nov 7, 1978. In the second of a two-part edition, Alan Whicker visits the city of Bombay, meeting some of its wealthiest residents, including film stars and industrialists and inhabitants of the shanty slum areas.

Alan Whicker Vintage Kathakali Footage https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=6uXVJpofJ5c

episodes.title: Erlendur Haraldsson talks about Sathya Sai Baba (2012)
episodes.description:
Erlendur Haraldsson talks about Sathya Sai Baba (2012) is an excerpt from An interview with Erlendur Haraldsson (2012) https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rEd0DGESEdc

Erlendur Haraldsson is professor emeritus of psychology at the University of Iceland. Studied philosophy at the universities of Iceland, Edinburg and Freiburg 1955-58. Writer and journalist 1959-63. Studied psychology at the University of Freiburg and University of Munich where he obtained the Dipl. Psych. in 1969. Research fellow at the Institute of Parapsychology in Durham, N. C. 1969-70, internship in clinical psychology at the Department of Psychiatry, University of Virginia in Charlottesville 1970-71. Ph.D. in psychology from the University of Freiburg in 1972. Research associate at the American Society for Psychical Research 1972-74. After that in the Department of Psychology at the University of Iceland. Visiting professor at the University of Virginia, 1982-83, and at the Institut für Grenzgebiete der Psychologie und Psychohygiene in Freiburg 1993-95. Dr. Haraldsson has published numerous scientific articles and book chapters, and is the author of  seven books, four of which have appeared in English, At the Hour of Death (with Karlis Osis), Modern Miracles, Sathya Sai Baba. The Story of a Modern Day Prophet,The Departed Among the Living,and Indridi Indridason, the Icelandic Physical Medium (with Loftur R. Gissurarson).

Erlendur Haraldsson is professor emeritus of psychology at the University of Iceland. Studied philosophy at the universities of Iceland, Edinburg and Freiburg 1955-58. Writer and journalist 1959-63. Studied psychology at the University of Freiburg and University of Munich where he obtained the Dipl. Psych. in 1969. Research fellow at the Institute of Parapsychology in Durham, N. C. 1969-70, internship in clinical psychology at the Department of Psychiatry, University of Virginia in Charlottesville 1970-71. Ph.D. in psychology from the University of Freiburg in 1972. Research associate at the American Society for Psychical Research 1972-74. After that in the Department of Psychology at the University of Iceland. Visiting professor at the University of Virginia, 1982-83, and at the Institut für Grenzgebiete der Psychologie und Psychohygiene in Freiburg 1993-95. Dr. Haraldsson has published numerous scientific articles and book chapters, and is the author of  seven books, four of which have appeared in English, At the Hour of Death (with Karlis Osis), Modern Miracles, Sathya Sai Baba. The Story of a Modern Day Prophet,The Departed Among the Living,and Indridi Indridason, the Icelandic Physical Medium (with Loftur R. Gissurarson).

episodes.title: The Bombay Ananda Bhavan Hotel
episodes.description:
A devotees hotel from the past, the Bombay Ananda Bhavan Hotel was an oasis set amongst the Raj-era bungalows in the cantonment of Bangalore, India. The Bangalore cantonment of bungalows, gardens, race courses, clubs, barracks and parade grounds for its mixed civilian and military population, was established in 1809, separate from the city.

The original address for this 12-room establishment built in 1920, was 12 Grant Road and then 10, before the renaming of the street, when it then became 68 Vittal Mallya Road. The guest’s name and country of origin were displayed in the lobby, so no trouble to see who was checked in. Situated in the quietest area of Bangalore, the guests could easily walk to MG Road, via Lavelle or St. Mark's Road to Koshy’s famous Bar and Restaurant. The Bombay Ananda Bhavan Hotel was demolished around 2005. In 2018, the Indian National Trust for Art and Cultural Heritage, stated that private buildings are the most poorly conserved in Bengaluru, in the past 30 years, the city has lost 75% of its heritage homes, with only 129 out of 510 surviving today.

Brindavan, in Whitefield (on the outskirts of Bangalore) is the second ashram. Sai Shruti in Kodaikanal, a hill station in the south of India, is the third ashram.

Ashrams of India https://ashramsofindia.com/ "A massive 1749 page two volume book set that explores over 500 ashrams, temples, and other significant sites of eastern religious and spiritual interest located throughout the Indian sub-continent. The compilers have backed up their explorations with reems of classical, historical, and recent background information." - The Wanderling https://the-wanderling.com/

Ashrams of India is a travel guide that explores over 500 places of spiritual interest for the modern traveller.

Westerners had been visiting the ashrams of saints since the early part of the twentieth century. Their stories and the places that they visited, many of which are still in existence, are gathered together here in 'Ashrams of India' with practical advice including contact details, directions, and coordinates that link to Google Maps.

episodes.title: Exploring The Religions Of South Asia : A Contemporary Guru : Rajnish (1975)
episodes.description:
Exploring The Religions Of South Asia. An educational television series of 15 episodes on Hinduism, Islam, Buddhism, Jainism, and Christianity in India, presented by David M. Knipe, historian of religions.

David M. Knipe PLAYLIST https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLfvx-cbEOmxAf33H7ZjDF9cs3MaklpMpe

The first documentary film about Rajneesh was made by David M. Knipe. Program 13 of Exploring The Religions Of South Asia, A Contemporary Guru : Rajnish. (Madison: WHA-TV 1974)

Examines the role of guru in Hinduism using Bhagwan Shree Rajneesh as a model. Devotees are interviewed, Rajneesh lectures presented, dancing and whirling are shown as examples of ecstatic expressions of devotion juxtaposed to silent meditation techniques, along with different forms of therapy.

WHA-TV public access in Madison, Wisconsin ran a series titled Exploring the Religions of South Asia. Episode 13 was titled ‘A Contemporary Guru: Rajnish’ and was primarily filmed at the newly established Shree Rajneesh Ashram in August of that year.

The entire series were recently uploaded to YouTube, including the episode dedicated to ‘Rajnish’, an interesting spelling variation of Rajneesh, later to be known as Osho. It is worth a look not only for its preciously rare footage of the ashram in its infancy, but also for the commentary on the movement given by the show’s host, Professor David M. Knipe of the University of Wisconsin.

The half-hour episode’s first ten minutes serves as an introduction to the various godmen and gurus who were sprouting in India at that time with an appeal to Western audiences, and of the various signs of devotion and ecstasy that they inspired in their disciples.

The program then focuses on ‘Rajnish’ as it reveals various scenes of activity at his ashram, which had been established only five months prior to filming. The morning discourse is shown, which at the time was still held in the somewhat crowded first-floor terrace of Lao Tzu house, prior to the construction of Chuang Tzu Auditorium; the lecture heard that morning was given on August 15, 1974 and appears as Chapter 5 of No Water, No Moon.

The documentary then moves on to show the various meditations happening throughout the day (the period from August 11-20 would have coincided with the ashram’s monthly meditation camp), including several that would soon be discontinued such as Mandala (aka “rolling eye”) and Kirtan; notable ashramites who can be seen in the footage include Veena and Madhuri. Divine Healing is then shown, before finishing that evening with Sufi Whirling, a graceful exercise which Professor Knipe appears particularly impressed with.

Knipe’s commentary is largely positive toward the movement, as he tries to find a link between what went on at the ashram with the experiences of devotion and ecstasy/catharsis discussed earlier in the program. Knipe does make one puzzling swipe at the master’s books, which he describes as “occasionally interesting, but the prose is clouded over with traditional bombast and misdirected metaphors,” an opinion sure to be vehemently contested.

What is undeniable, however, is that this earliest of looks at the ashram – when it was still comprised of just two houses, largely Indian in sannyasin composition, and devoid of therapy groups or any real controversy – is historic, coming some 16 months prior to Robert Hillman’s Bhagwan and over four years away from Ashram in Poona.

The footage of Mandala, Kirtan, Divine Healing and Sufi Whirling is the only film that survives of these techniques as they were practiced in Pune 1 and make for engrossing viewing.

episodes.title: Suri Bhagavantam and Sathya Sai Baba
episodes.description:
Suri Bhagavantam and Sathya Sai Baba.

00:00 Suri Bhagavantam appears in the 1990 Swiss documentary 'Who is Sai Baba?' 
by Victor J. Tognola https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2FhqPZb-3V8&ab_channel=AshramsofIndia
06:43 From Richard Bock’s 1973 film ‘Advent of the Avatar’  Suri Bhagavantam is with 
Pandit Ravi Shankar, Ustad Allah Rakha Khan in an interview with Sathya Sai Baba in the Brindavan Ashram, 1972 https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jX8TuK5c6GM&ab_channel=AshramsofIndia
08:13 Suri Bhagavantam translates for Sathya Sai Baba
09:52 Suri Bhagavantam appears in ‘Sathya Sai Baba’ (November, 1975) https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=h26nqN08MX0&ab_channel=AshramsofIndia
10:43 Fred Greenberg interviews Suri Bhagavantam, Howard Murphet, Don Heath, Sam Sandweiss, 
and Larry Smith in Prasanthi Nilayam, Christmas time in 1978. Video by Jack Lenchiner https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=NH2AQkffOFQ&ab_channel=AshramsofIndia

It may be surprising to many people - though in fact it should not be - to find that a scientist of the calibre of Dr. S. Bhagavantam, M.Sc., Ph.D., D.Sc., is a devoted follower of an Adept in that field of high transcendental magic which science tends to scorn.

Dr Bhagavantam, formerly Director of the All India Institute of Science, holds the prominent position of Scientific Advisor to the Ministry of Defence in Delhi, and is well-known in scientific circles outside India.

When I met him at Prasanthi Nilayam he was occupying a room furnished only with two bed-rolls and a few cushions on the floor. Like all good Indians he was quite happy to use the tiled floor as bedstead, chair and table. With him in the same room was one of his sons, Dr. S. Balakrishna, Assistant Director of the National Geophysical Research Institute of India. Both were visiting the ashram for a few days.

I sat on the floor with these two cultured scientists and charming gentlemen, anxious to hear of their experiences with Sai Baba. Outside the open door and windows the July sun gleamed on the sandy soil, white buildings and rocky hills. Inside Dr. Bhagavantam spoke in is quiet, friendly, concise way, while his son confirmed many of the strange events which he too had witnessed. Dr. Balakrishna has had some wonderful experiences of his own with Baba, but here we are concerned with the remarkable reports from his eminent father.

At Dr. Bhagavantam's first meeting with Sai Baba, which was in the year 1959, they went for a walk on the sands of the Chitravati River.  Others were present, but Bhagavantam was walking by the side of Baba.

After a while Swami asked him to select a place on the sands for sitting down. When the doctor hesitated, Baba insisted, explaining that only in this way could Bhagavantam's scientific mind be quite sure that Baba had not led him to a spot where an object had been "planted" in the sands.

After the scientist had chosen an area and the party was seated on the sands, Baba began to tease the doctor a little; he made fun of the complacent "all-knowing" attitude of many men of science, and deplored their ignorance of or indifference to the ancient wisdom to be found in the great Hindu scriptures.

The doctor’s pride was stung. He retorted that not all scientists were of this materialistic outlook. He himself, as an example, had a family tradition of Sanskrit learning and a deep interest in the spiritual classics of India.
Then in an endeavour to establish the bona fides of his scientific colleagues he told Baba that when Oppenheimer, after exploding the first atom bomb, was asked by the press representatives what his reactions were, he replied by quoting a verse from the Bhagavad Gita, thus showing that he was a student of that great work.

"Would you like a copy of the Bhagavad Gita?" Baba asked him suddenly, scooping up a handful of sand as he spoke. "Here it is," he continued, "hold out your hands."

Bhagavantam cupped his hands to catch the sand as Baba dropped it into them. But when it reached the scientist's waiting palms, it was no longer the golden sand of the Chitravati. It was a red-covered book.

Opening it in stunned silence, the doctor found that it was a copy of the Bhagavad Gita printed in Telegu script. Baba remarked that he could have presented the doctor with one printed in Sanskrit, but as the latter read Sanskrit script with some difficulty, Baba had given him one in Telegu, Bhagavantam's native tongue. Bhagavantam had not mentioned his limited proficiency in Sanskrit, this was something that Baba just knew.

As soon as he could, Bhagavantam examined this miraculously produced volume closely. It appeared to be quite new and was well-printed, but where? The names of printer and publisher, always given in the normal way, were nowhere to be found.

From "Sai Baba Man of Miracles" by Howard Murphet.

episodes.title: Arnaud Desjardins talks about his experiences with Ma Anandamayi
episodes.description: Sadly Arnaud Desjardins just passed away on 10th August. He first met Ma Anandamayi in 1959, when at the time he has a film and television producer in France. He has produced several films on spirituality, and the one on Sri Ma, that was shown on French TV,  was an eye-opener for many interested in genuine spirituality.  He also refers to Swami Prajnanpad, who he met around that time. This talk was given at a retreat at St Privat on 25th May 2011 to devotees of Ma Anandamayi who had assembled there. This video is posted with the kind permission of Arnaud Desjardins.

episodes.title: The Jack Hislop Tapes
episodes.description:
The Jack Hislop Tapes

00:00 Intro
00:21 Arriving in Prasanthi Nilayam, January, 1968

The subject of abortion https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=6Qru78GHmD4&ab_channel=AshramsofIndia

Among the first Americans to follow  Maharishi Mahesh Yogi was John Hislop, a land developer and erstwhile Theosophist, who had been a follower of Krishnamurti and Yogananda before discovering the Maharishi, and who would later become a devotee of Sathya Sai Baba. John and Victoria Hislop first meet Baba in January 1968, at his ashram Prasanthi Nilayam.

The first visit of Dr John (Jack) Hislop (1904-1995) and his wife Victoria, to Sathya Sai Baba was in January 1968, after being told of the guru of Puttaparthi by Diana Baskin’s mother, Mrs Annalisa Rajagopalacharya. They stayed 10 days.

He taped two deeply spiritual conversations with Baba who, he immediately felt, was the true source of wisdom. At that time there were only a few foreigners in Puttaparthi and Hislop was especially privileged to have daily meetings with Sathya Sai Baba and receive visits from him, lasting up to two hours per day (My Baba and I, p.15). The results of these long conversations (mainly question and answer sessions) were later published in California (in 1978) as Conversations with Sathya Sai Baba, a book which offered many invaluable insights into Baba’s teachings and revealed to foreign devotees and others his opinions on many topics. As a result of many further visits and periods of residence in the ashram, Dr Hislop later wrote My Baba and I, which, like the first book, has been instrumental in the US and around the world in increasing knowledge about Sathya Sai Baba and drawing devotees to him (especially foreigners).

Jack and his wife were prominent devotees.

episodes.title: The Five Masters of Meher Baba
episodes.description:
The Five Masters of Meher Baba (1998)

00:00 Hazrat Babajan
03:49 Tajuddin Baba
06:00 Narayan Maharaj
07:43 Sai Baba of Shirdi
09:08 Upasani Maharaj https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=tlLfkt-lxpc&ab_channel=AshramsofIndia

Meher Baba PLAYLIST https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLfvx-cbEOmxBqsyWXiYlakpFkg9ltYynZ

Meher Baba was born in Poona, India in 1894. His mission started in 1921 and developed as a practical example of his principle message of love. He worked with disciples in India, Europe, America and the poor of India, through free schools, dispensaries and shelters. His life was devoted not to teaching but to awakening humanity to the unifying message of love and the Truth given by great messengers of the past. His belief that all had been said was given emphasis by his observing silence for 44 years, from 1925 until his death in 1969. 'Meher Baba, The Awakener' 1994, 2018.

Meher Baba : His Life, Work and Message https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=IJViIOb64c4&ab_channel=AshramsofIndia

episodes.title: The Secret Cave in India : Patal Bhuvaneshwar (1995)
episodes.description:
Ashrams of India Volume 2 : Patal Bhuvaneshwar Cave : Cave that is said to be the dwelling place of Shiva and his consort Pavarti. Pilgrims today recount visionary experiences, including seeing great light beings.

Caves and Guhas https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLfvx-cbEOmxBBQdASWwRn8h9JwBe-PdbQ

Patal Bhuvaneshwar, literally means the subterranean shrine of Lord Shiva. It is a limestone cave temple at 1350 m, 14 km from Gangolihat in the Pithoragarh district of Uttarakhand. It is located 500 m from the village of Bhuvneshwar. It is believed that Shankaracharya also visited this place in 1191 AD. There is an inscription on the entrance, dating back to the 14th century AD. This underground cave enshrines Lord Shiva and 33 crore Gods (330 million), one theory is that the number of 330 million gods refers to the total count of the then known population of all the humans and living beings that ever walked the Earth, including the 8.4 million jeeva rasi (living species) signifying that god exists in every living being.

Interviews with Sathya Sai Baba https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLfvx-cbEOmxBOphkiR_QPIHgbyR_Xs9Jz

Ashrams of India Volume 1 & 2 : Prasanthi Nilayam, in Puttaparthi, Andhra Pradesh (125 km NNE of Bangalore) is Sathya Sai Baba's main ashram. Brindavan, in Whitefield (on the outskirts of Bangalore) is the second ashram. Kodaikanal, a hill station in the south of India, is where the third ashram is, Sai Shruti.

episodes.title: Ranganathan Padmanaban and Sathya Sai Baba
episodes.description:
This interview with Ranganathan Padmanaban (Pad) was conducted by Ted Henry and Jody Cleary. It is an edited excerpt from https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=kR5NPvBsZQY&t=641s&ab_channel=TedHenry

00:00 Love Is My Form by R. Padmanaban. Love is My Form. Vol. 1. The Advent (1926-1950). (Bangalore, Sai Towers Publishing, 2000, edited by R. Padmanaban, ISBN 81-86-8227-71). The research had also included the gathering of material from 140 tape recorded interviews with elderly local people who had known Baba in the earlier years (see the list on pp. 578-579). The volume also included 759 priceless photographs (many seen for the first time and many carefully dated). These had been painstakingly gathered and presented by Pad, with a special “warm grey and a layer of spot varnish” to revive the dusty and damaged old black and white photos dating back to 1940 (but mainly from 1943 on). The book also offers 61 illustrations, 43 documents and 4 maps.
04:08 Pad as a Photographer for Sathya Sai Baba
14:17 Pad has unlimited access to Sathya Sai Baba
15:32 Work...
15:48 Devotion...
19:10 Sai Towers. One of the signs that Baba devotee numbers worldwide had reached critical mass in the early 1990s was the opening of a very new type of business venture, the highly efficient city-style Sai Towers building. Set on a prime position of the main street of the growing but still rudimentary township of Puttaparthi, Sai Towers occupied a large modern-style building which offered a superior style café-hotel-bookshop and publishing house. It quickly became the best known and most visited Puttaparthi business, thanks mainly to an enthusiastic clientele of visiting or mail-shopping foreign devotees. A second branch was later opened in Whitefield. Sai Towers became famous for the quality binding and paper of its devotee books about Baba.

episodes.title: Antonio Rigopoulos in conversation with Narayana Kasturi (1985) (AUDIO)
episodes.description:
Antonio Rigopoulos in conversation with Narayana Kasturi (1985) is an excerpt from The Kasturi Tapes https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Q6i5Da4McD8&ab_channel=AshramsofIndia

Narayana Kasturi (25 December 1897 – 14 August 1987) was an Indian writer, professor, and journalist.

00:00 Intro
00:12 Photos
03:14 Sathya Sai Baba and Kasturi ~ Vibhuti Abhishek for Shirdi Sai Baba in the Poornachandra Hall, Prasanthi Nilayam. Filmed by Richard Bock. Richard Bock Films https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLfvx-cbEOmxCJUpc0d5SuUSwLf8JprVXu
04:51 The Hagiographer and the Avatar: The Life and Works of Narayan Kasturi by Antonio Rigopoulos
05:26 Antonio Rigopoulos in conversation with Narayana Kasturi in Kasturi’s apartment, Prasanthi Nilayam, 5:15 pm on Friday, November 8th, 1985 (AUDIO)
44:36 Professor Narayana Kasturi talks about Vibhuti https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2FhqPZb-3V8&ab_channel=AshramsofIndia
46:42 Professor Narayana Kasturi sees Sathya Sai Baba as a Poem

Kasturi authored the work 'Sathyam Sivam Sundaram,' which was translated into English as 'Truth, Goodness, Beauty'. The first part of the book was published in 1960 and covers the first 34 years of the life of Sathya Sai Baba. Kasturi stated that he gathered information for the book from various sources, including Sathya Sai Baba's father and brother, as well as teachers from the schools he attended. The second part of the book was published in 1968, and the complete work was published around 1980. 

Narayana Kasturi was a writer and journalist who wrote for the magazine Koravanji in the 1940s and 1950s. He was known for discovering and promoting the work of young writers. He also wrote hagiographic accounts of Sathya Sai Baba's life and his own autobiography, entitled "Loving God: Eighty-five Years under the Watchful Eye of the Lord" (1982).

episodes.title: The Kasturi Tapes
episodes.description:
Narayana Kasturi (25 December 1897 – 14 August 1987) was an Indian writer, professor, and journalist.

EXTENDED VERSION OF THIS VIDEO https://youtu.be/Q6i5Da4McD8?si=bDz3yAvgzjEHsZHE

00:00 Intro
00:12 Photos
00:21 Sathya Sai Baba and Kasturi ~ Vibhuti Abhishek for Shirdi Sai Baba in the Poornachandra Hall, Prasanthi Nilayam. Filmed by Richard Bock. Richard Bock Films https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLfvx-cbEOmxCJUpc0d5SuUSwLf8JprVXu
01:58 Professor Narayana Kasturi talks about Vibhuti https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2FhqPZb-3V8&ab_channel=AshramsofIndia
03:27 Professor Narayana Kasturi talks to Westerners. 1983.
01:04:58 Professor Narayana Kasturi talks about the Annunciation Poem. 20th December, 1983. Filmed by Jack Lenchiner. With English Subtittles https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=O-_aFlHf-7M&t=624s&ab_channel=TedHenry
02:00:47 The Annunciation Poem
02:11:56 Professor Narayana Kasturi talks to Westerners. 28th December, 1984.
03:16:28 Outro ~ Ashrams of India

Kasturi authored the work 'Sathyam Sivam Sundaram,' which was translated into English as 'Truth, Goodness, Beauty'. The first part of the book was published in 1960 and covers the first 34 years of the life of Sathya Sai Baba. Kasturi stated that he gathered information for the book from various sources, including Sathya Sai Baba's father and brother, as well as teachers from the schools he attended. The second part of the book was published in 1968, and the complete work was published around 1980. 

Narayana Kasturi was a writer and journalist who wrote for the magazine Koravanji in the 1940s and 1950s. He was known for discovering and promoting the work of young writers. He also wrote hagiographic accounts of Sathya Sai Baba's life and his own autobiography, entitled "Loving God: Eighty-five Years under the Watchful Eye of the Lord" (1982).

ASHRAMS OF INDIA Recommended Books (https://ashramsofindia.com/recommended-books/books-m-to-s/) Seeking the Master: Muz Murray

This book was the first ever guide to the ashrams of India.

First published in 1980. It became renowned as the “Bible of India-bound spiritual seekers.” The Gurus and their teachings explored in this work have long left this worldly plane, but the ashrams are still there with new teachers and the directions of how to find them are invaluable. It contains information on over 300 ashrams and monasteries, their daily programmes and teachings, with the impressions of Western seekers of both the Masters and the ashrams of the time, plus a wealth of geographical and travel information, tips on keeping healthy in India and much more. It’s worth taking a look.

Available from: (https://www.muzmurray.com/product-page/seeking-the-master-ebook-download-pdf)

episodes.title: Na Rubu Znanosti (On the Edge of Science) ~ Sai Baba with Vesna Krmpotić
episodes.description:
Vesna Krmpotić (17 June 1932 – 21 August 2018) was a Croatian writer and translator.

Born in Dubrovnik, Krmpotić graduated from the University of Zagreb with a degree in English language and psychology. In New Delhi, India, she studied the Bengali language. After marrying Radivoje Petković, a diplomat, Krmpotić has lived in Cairo, Washington, Accra, New Delhi, and since 2004 she resided in Belgrade.[2] She wrote over 70 books and the book Brdo iznad oblaka (A Hill Above the Clouds, 1987) is considered to be one of the best Croatian novels. Krmpotić had an interest in oriental religion and philosophy, being particularly influenced by the teaching of Sathya Sai Baba.

Krmpotić held many literary awards given in Croatia, including the Vladimir Nazor Award in 1999 and Tin Ujević Award in 2013.

episodes.title: Lucas Ralli Interview (1995)
episodes.description:
Lucas Ralli is interviewed by Roy Creek (1995).

Books by Lucas Ralli ~ Sai Messages For You and Me: Volumes I, II, III, IV

It was not until January 1981 that I made my first pilgrimage to find Bhagavan, although by this time I was in daily contact with Him in meditation and through the messages. We spent two and a half weeks in Bangalore as Bhagavan was at Brindavan, and we attended darshan there twice a day. I was longing for an interview, but although Bhagavan looked at me and right through me on several occasions, there was no interview. Then Bhagavan left for Madras and was due to go on to Bombay. In a message, He told me to go and wait for Him in Bombay which is what we did. Then He arrived and we had the most wonderful darshans at the Dharmakshetra. Again Bhagavan looked at me and right through me. And on the last morning, just before He left Bombay, He called our little group of four people for an interview. Once inside the interview room, time became timeless and it seemed as though Bhagavan enjoyed playing with us, His children, and would give us anything we wanted. The whole atmosphere was utterly divine.

We returned to London but knew that we would be back in India before the end of the year. And in October 1981, we arrived at Puttaparthi just in time for the Dasara festival. What an incredible experience it was. Once again Bhagavan looked at me, sometimes looking right into me or right through me, and at other times giving me that divine smile which melts the heart. Then one day, without warning, I discovered that I was in the "repair shop"! Bhagavan totally ignored me, and did it in such a way that there could not be any doubt about what was happening. One day when I was in the front row, Bhagavan came out of the Mandir and walked very slowly straight towards me. He seemed to be looking straight at me and I thought I was in for some wonderful experience. So I was! He stopped right in front of me and then called in for interview everyone round me. But I was ignored and found myself sitting in a sort of void. What a message!

episodes.title: Richard Bock Films : AURA OF DIVINITY Parts 1 and 2 (1980)
episodes.description:
Richard Bock Films https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLfvx-cbEOmxCJUpc0d5SuUSwLf8JprVXu

Richard Bock joined Indra Devi in India for Baba’s forty-third birthday in November 1968, and returned with eight millimetre film and the recordings of Baba which became the LP entitled, Sathya Sai Baba Chants the Bhajans.

Richard Bock was the Founder-President of Pacific Jazz and World Pacific Records. Through a long personal and professional relationship with Pandit Ravi Shankar, the world renowned sitar maestro, Mr. Bock was instrumental in introducing classical Indian music to large audiences in the West. episodes.title: Imram Kriya talks about Sathya Sai Baba
episodes.description:
Who are the Avatars and when do they come to Earth? How many Avatars are known to mankind and who are they? 
Imram talks about Sathya Sai Baba is from https://www.youtube.com/@IMRAMKRIYA-YOGA
Who is Sai Baba, what is his power and why he came in the form of a human being. 
What are the qualities of an Avatar.
How you can communicate with Sai Baba.
Why Sai Baba took letters from people with requests. 
What are the differences between the Avatars Lahiri Mahasaya, Sri Yukteswar and Sathya Sai Baba. 
How objects materialise. 
Where are all the things that Sai Baba materialised. 
Are Sai Baba's photos an amulet?
Why Avatars also get sick. 
Imram shares his experience of meeting Sathya Sai Baba, amazing stories of miraculous blessings and stories of the Avatar's sense of humour. 

00:00 - Who was Sai Baba? 
12:17 - Sai Baba advice
14:57 - Why did Sai Baba take letters from people with requests? 
17:32 - Why did we miss His coming? 
21:01 - Where are all the things that Sai Baba materialised? 
22:40 - What is the difference between the Avatars Lahiri Mahasaya, Sri Yukteswar and Sathya Sai Baba? 
24:26 - Immersion and Oneness with the Absolute
25:01 - Practices are never ending
26:54 - What was the sacred meaning of the Avatar breaking his legs
27:38 - Are pictures of Sai Baba an amulet?

Sathya Sai Baba. The Reality of God / Imram Giri and Svyatoslav Dubyansky https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=y6U1jNucg_E&t=1450s&ab_channel=ImramKriya-Yoga

episodes.title: Writing a Letter to Sathya Sai Baba
episodes.description:
Lyn Kriegler reveals the profound results of writing to Sathya Sai Baba ~ posting the letter and what is happening in higher dimensions.

Lyn is a long-time devotee of Sathya Sai Baba, having her first darshan with Baba in 1989. Subsequently she received many interviews and gained numerous experiences from Baba. 

She has served in organisations such as the Books and Publication Trust, Radio Sai, SSSMC Easwaramma Women’s Welfare Trust, and the NZ Sai Book Centre and Lending Library. She is also a book illustrator, scriptwriter, and oral storyteller. Originally from the United States, her career as a staff artist and art director involved working for various organizations such as Mademoiselle, The New Yorker, and The Washington Post. Kriegler immigrated to New Zealand in 1974.

episodes.title: Sathya Sai Baba ~ Master & Avatar, 1976
episodes.description:
In 1976, Prof. Lawrence Galante, had a dream to change his Master's Thesis in Comparative Religions from that on Saint Teresa of Avila, and instead to write his thesis on Sathya Sai Baba. Skeptical that the stories he had heard of Sai Baba's miracles might be made up by his devotees, and having suddenly and mysteriously received and unexpected $2,000 (enough for a plane trip to India),  Dr. Galante made a three month visit to Sai Baba's ashram to get the facts about this mysterious person first hand and for himself.

Every day, Lawrence watched Sai Baba tirelessly attending to the masses, materialising vibhuti and religious objects, and then giving them away as gifts to devotees. Every day, Baba healed the sick and taught something to everyone who came to him.

This is the film of that trip, shot by L. Galante on a Super 8 camera. Most of the narrated dialogue, are direct quotes spoken and written by Sathya Sai Baba. It contains rare and revealing scenes of the daily life of Sai Baba and a rare view of authentic materialisations. Called by the London Times "the most impressive holy man to emerge in hundreds of years," Sai Baba remains an enigma for all humankind to ponder.

See another film about Lawrence Galante ~ A Christian's Pilgrimage To The Master ~ Sathya Sai Baba by Lawrence Galante https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1XL0RtqFcqE&ab_channel=AshramsofIndia

episodes.title: Gurudeva Nityananda of Ganeshpuri
episodes.description:
Gurudeva Nityananda of Ganeshpuri filmed by M.D. Suvarna.

The Suvarna family ~ M.D. Suvarna was a press photographer with Illustrated Weekly of India. In mid-1954 he was introduced to Bhagavan Nityananda of Ganeshpuri. He became one of his disciples and Bhagavan gave him his blessing to take photos of him, as long as he never asked him to pose. Sri Suvarna took all of the famous photographs of Bhagavan Nityananda and also shot a 16mm film and recorded his voice.

His son, Niranjan, follows in his father’s footsteps as a devotee of Bhagavan Nityananda. He runs his father’s Foto Corner in Mumbai and has produced beautiful collections of books and photos of Bhagavan Nityananda. Recently, he produced a 3 hour documentary on Bhagavan Nityananda’s life called Sadguru Nityananda.

Nityananda https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLfvx-cbEOmxAbhLH7_-z1V3qv4KJWlTnB

Ashrams of India Volume 1 ~ Bhagavan Nityananda ashrams

•Nityananda Ashram 
•Guruvan

Ashrams of India : Volume 1 : According to Kashmiri folklore, Jesus survived the crucifixion, left the Middle East for Kashmir, arriving in 54 AD aged 60, where he married and died at the age of 120 and is buried in the Roza Bal shrine in Srinagar. Roza Bal is the name of a shrine located at the corner of a narrow lane in Anzimar, next to a small Muslim cemetery in the Khanyaar quarter of the old part of Srinagar in Kashmir. Khanyaar is in central Srinagar 4 km away from Lal Chowk. The word Roza Bal is derived from the Kashmir term Rauza-Bal which means “tomb of the prophet.” Jesus and Moses in Kashmir https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Mw60a11ywrc&ab_channel=AshramsofIndia

episodes.title: Elizabeth Clare Prophet and Hinduism
episodes.description:
00:00 Hinduism Part 1 - Fount of World Mysticism : Introduction to one of the oldest religions in the world. Mystical truths within Hinduism.
04:29 Hinduism Part 2 - Om, Brahman, and the Atman : Definition of the Om and begins to define Brahman and the Atman.
07:55 Hinduism Part 3 - Differentiating Atman and Soul : The concepts of soul and Spirit as correlating with the Hindu concepts of Jiva and Atman.
11:21 Hinduism Part 4 - Free Will of the Soul : because the soul has free will it can decide to either become immortal by uniting with the Atman or it can deny both the Atman and Brahman and ultimately die.
17:41 Hinduism Part 5 – Ramana Maharshi : Henri Le Saux and the life of Ramana Maharshi, who was one of the great Hindu Mystics. Abhishiktananda, born Henri Le Saux, was a French-born Indian monk. He moved to India in 1948 in search of a more radical form of spiritual life, adopted sannyasa in accordance with Indian tradition and became one of the pioneers of Hindu-Christian dialogue. Ramana Maharshi's Western visitors from 1911 to 1950  https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=RI_v_dcKY-I&ab_channel=AshramsofIndia
20:53 Hinduism Part 6 - The Fish and the Yogi : Brahman is within you and you are within Brahman. Elisabeth Clare Prophet speaks about the oneness with God described in the Upanishads.
24:34 Hinduism Part 7 - Rending the Veil of Ignorance : Elizabeth Clare Prophet reads from the Atma Bodha about the veil of ignorance created by our own negative thoughts and feelings in this and past embodiments.
27:20 Hinduism Part 8 - Abiding in the Heart of Brahman : Two stories depicting Yogis and their students learning to be one with Brahman which is within all of life, and two mantras used for meditations on your own oneness with God.
34:46 Hinduism Part 9 – Visualising oneness with God : Techniques for meditating on your own oneness with Brahman.

episodes.description:

DOWNLOAD other books by Zecharia Sitchin https://avalonlibrary.net/ebooks/

Over the years, startling evidence has been unearthed, challenging established notions of the origins of Earth and life on it and suggesting the existence of a superior race of beings who once inhabited our world. The product of thirty years of intensive research, The 12th Planet is the first book in Zecharia Sitchin's prophetic Earth Chronicles series - a revolutionary body of work that offers indisputable documentary proof of humanity's extraterrestrial forefathers. Travelers from the stars, they arrived eons ago, and planted the genetic seed that would ultimately blossom into a remarkable species... called Man.

The 12th Planet brings to life the Sumerian civilization, presenting millennia-old evidence of the existence of Nibiru, the home planet of the Anunnaki and of the landings of the Anunnaki on Earth every 3,600 years, and reveals a complete history of the solar system as told by these early visitors from another planet.

Zecharia Sitchin's Earth Chronicles series, with millions of copies sold worldwide, deal with the history and prehistory of Earth and humankind. Each book in the series is based upon information written on clay tablets by the ancient civilizations of the Near East. The series is offered here, for the first time, in highly readable, hardbound collector's editions with enhanced maps and diagrams.

Zecharia Sitchin (1920 - 2010) was born in Russia and grew up in Palestine, where he acquired a profound knowledge of modern and ancient Hebrew, other Semitic and European languages, the Old Testament, and the history and archaeology of the Near East. A graduate of the University of London with a degree in economic history, he worked as a journalist and editor for many years prior to undertaking his life’s work - The Earth Chronicles.

One of the few scholars able to read the clay tablets and interpret ancient Sumerian and Akkadian, Sitchin based The Earth Chronicles series on the texts and pictorial evidence recorded by the ancient civilizations of the Near East. His books have been widely translated, reprinted in paperback editions, converted to Braille for the blind, and featured on radio and television programs.

episodes.title: Timothy Leary's 1964 'trip' to India and his and Meher Baba's ideas about LSD
episodes.description:
Timothy Leary's 1964 'trip' to India and an excerpt from the 2012 film Nema Aviona Za Zagreb, directed by Louis Van Gasteren https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=E7BhnI81P8w&t=3517s&ab_channel=ChristopherOtt The segment quadrangulates between Gasteren's personal experience with legal use of LSD for therapy in the early 60s, the tragic death of a boy falling out a San Francisco apartment building on LSD, believing he could fly, Timothy Leary pontificating on LSD's spiritual benefits, and Meher Baba's message on the ultimate effects of drugs being madness. The feature length self-reflexive documentary was shot from 1966 to 1969, but wasn't completed until 2012. 

Winter 1964, following in his friend Allen Ginberg's footsteps, Timothy Leary finally set off for the East, travelling with his new wife Nena. In Delhi, Leary and Nena met up with Ralph Metzner, his form Harvard colleague and collaborator, who had spent three months in Almora, studying with the Tibetan Buddhist philosopher Lama Anagarika Govinda. It was the end of winter when Leary, Nena and Metzner arrived at Almora. Each afternoon they made their way to Lama Govinda’s house for Buddhist instruction and meditation, and to listen to the elderly German. After four months in Almora, Leary and Nena’s romantic honeymoon was turning sour. Making enquiries locally about where they could find a guru who could teach them the mysteries of tantric sex, they were directed to the dwelling of a man named Sri Krishna Prem. In fact Krishna Prem was the last person who could offer any useful advice on subject, but he was the closest that Leary would come in India finding himself a guru.

episodes.title: I Am The One Reality ~ Meher Baba
episodes.description:
The film I AM THE ONE REALITY was inspired by the discovery of rare and never-before-seen film footage of Meher Baba taken by Elizabeth Patterson in 1933, in India and Italy. Part One tells the story of the Western women disciples' first trip to India to be with Baba and to meet His Eastern women disciples. Part Two is of Baba's second visit to Portofino, Italy, later that year, with a group of Western and Eastern disciples. The 35mm footage is interwoven with photos and excerpts from the diaries of Elizabeth, Kitty Davy, Margaret Craske, and Delia DeLeon. This video captures the intimacy of those early days with the Master when He drew His Western followers to Him with His extraordinary beauty and introduced them to what life with a spiritual master meant.

Directed by Wendy Haynes Connor
Produced by Wendy Haynes Connor and Buz Connor
Co-Directed & Edited By Luke Wolbach
Written By Buz Connor

©2012 EliNor Publications, Inc

Originally posted on https://www.youtube.com/@SheriarFoundation

Sathya Sai Vahini Part 2 (AUDIO) Chapters 13 to 24 https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=LSddo0tph5U&ab_channel=AshramsofIndia

Synopsis;
Bhagawan has announced Himself as the Divine Teacher of Truth, Beauty, and Goodness. By precept and example, through His writings and discourses, letters and conversations, He has been instilling the supreme wisdom and instructing all mankind to translate it into righteous living, inner peace, and universal love. When the Ramakatha Rasavahini, the uniquely authentic, nectarine stream of the Rama’s story, was serialised in full in the Sanathana Sarathi, Bhagawan blessed readers with a new series, which He named Bharatiya Paramartha Vahini (The Stream of Indian Spiritual Values). While these precious essays, on the basic truths that foster and feed Indian culture since ages before history began, were being published, Bhagawan decided to continue the flow of illumination and instruction under a more comprehensive and meaningful name, Sathya Sai Vahini - the Ganga from the Lotus Feet of the Lord - “The Flow of Divine Sai Grace.” This book, therefore, contains the two Vahinis that have merged in one master stream.

Inaugurating these series, Bhagawan wrote for publication in the Sanathana Sarathi, “Moved by the urge to cool the heat of conflict and to quench the agonising thirst for ‘knowledge about yourself’ that you are afflicted with, see, here it comes, the Sathya Sai Vahini, wave behind wave, with the Sanathana Sarathi as the medium between you and Me.” With infinite compassion, this Sathya Sai incarnation of the Omniwill is giving millions of persons in all lands freedom from disease, distress, and despair, narcotics, narcissism, and nihilism. He is encouraging those, who suffer gloom through wilful blindness, to light the Lamp of Love in order to see the world and the Lamp of Wisdom to see themselves. “This is a tantalising, true-false world. Its apparent diversity is an illusion. It is One, but is cognised by the maimed, multiple vision of humans as Many,” says Bhagawan. This book is the twin Lamp He has devised for us.

Gayathri Mantra for Daily Meditation.
"Gayathri Mantra" is one of the most powerful mantras connoting the quintessence of the Vedas. Gayathri has the power to relieve us of all diseases. It wards off all misery. Gayathri is the bestower of all that is beneficial. It is the fulfiller of all desires. If this great Mantra is chanted with purity of body and mind with correct intonation, one will be bestowed with Divine powers. episodes.title: Mitchell Rabin interviews Sai Maa in NYC (2003)
episodes.description:
Mitchell Rabin interviews Sai Maa in NYC (2003) is from https://www.youtube.com/@abetterworldtvshow/videos

Sai Maa (birth name unknown), also known as Sai Maa Lakshmi Devi Mishra, was born into a Hindu family on the island of Mauritius on August 2, 1953. She had spiritually transformative experiences during her childhood. At the age of 21, Sai Maa moved to France, where she would later marry a research scientist and become a mother of two children. While in France, Sai Maa acquired certifications in naturopathic, homeopathic and osteopathic therapies. She ran a private practice which combined alternative healing methods with her Western therapeutic training. Also active in politics, Sai Maa served for several years on the Bordeaux City Council, helping to reform health care in the European Union on behalf of the French government. In her late twenties, Sai Maa met and became a disciple of the spiritual leader, Sathya Sai Baba. She travelled frequently to Puttaparthi, India, to study with Sai Baba. While maintaining an active career and family life, Sai Maa focused on practising the teachings and disciplines she learned from Sai Baba and continues to praise Sai Baba to this day. In an interview, Sai Maa asserted that "Jesus, St Germain and the archangel Michael showed up in her room one day and told her it was time to 'be in the world,' and she left France and began her spiritual path."

Sai Maa ~ What Happens When You Meet A Master https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=04YRTFK6Z74&ab_channel=AshramsofIndia

episodes.title: Al Druker recalls the Interview with Sathya Sai Baba in Kodaikanal in April, 1985
episodes.description:
Al Druker recalls the Interview with Sathya Sai Baba in Kodaikanal in April, 1985 that was given to a group of foreigners who had come for darshan during Baba's first visit to Kodaikanal. Baba spoke of the Non-dual Advaita Vedanta as the heart of his teachings, and he answered many questions posed by the participants, couching his powerful presentation in easily understandable words that everyone could follow. He spoke without an interpreter directly in English. Unfortunately there were no video or even still cameras present.

Al Drucker first met Sathya Sai Baba in 1973 in Prasanthi Nilayam. Drucker compiled and edited Sai Baba Gita, published in 1993.

Al Drucker, interviewed in 1989 for the 1990 documentary The Way To Baba: A Pilgrimage to Prasanthi Nilayam by Elda Hartley https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gew7DsGBrbw&ab_channel=AshramsofIndia

episodes.title: The Miracle of Puttaparthi (1994) (higher quality film)
episodes.description:
The Miracle of Puttaparthi (1994) (this is a higher quality film than previously posted on this channel).

This documentary is about the Super Specialty Hospital in Puttaparthi. On the 23rd of November 1990, Sathya Sai Baba announced the construction of a new hospital and that it would be designed and built in one year, and immediately upon completion the first operation would take place.

Another documentary is about the Super Specialty Hospital in Puttaparthi ~ The Hospital Video (1993) https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=SG8mgaGHoo4&ab_channel=AshramsofIndia

Louise Illig-Mooncie https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1cRhwHNSsNA&ab_channel=AshramsofIndia

As Sathya Sai Baba said, "this hospital was established with the noble aim of serving suffering humanity." It serves mainly the poor and is entirely free of cost.

The Sathya Sai Institute of Higher Medical Sciences opened on the 22nd of November 1991. It is located in Prasanthi Gram, close to the Sri Sathya Sai Airport, which is about 9km from the ashram (Prasanthi Nilayam) and Puttaparthi. The hospital provides free heart, kidney, and eye surgery. 

Isaac Tigrett, who founded the Hard Rock Cafe enterprise, secured Professor Keith Critchlow's expertise to design this hospital. His use of sacred geometry played a major role in many architectural designs and projects.

Isaac Tigrett Tapes https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=TEn8lcvTR-0&ab_channel=AshramsofIndia

Chartres Cathedral ~ A Sacred Geometry ~ Professor Keith Critchlow https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JEiBypewW48&ab_channel=AshramsofIndia

Glastonbury ~ Professor Keith Critchlow (1970) https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=wIrjZVRgLV4&ab_channel=AshramsofIndia

Saint Gregory ~ Professor Keith Critchlow https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-7tI4WslEZw&ab_channel=AshramsofIndia

The Chakras ~ Professor Keith Critchlow https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=RurimSj0LKk&ab_channel=AshramsofIndia

episodes.title: Miracles of Sathya Sai Baba in Connecticut (1982)
episodes.description:
This video was filmed by Jack Lenchiner in Sai Nivas, which is now known as the Shelton Sai Centre, and shows the miracles of Sathya Sai Baba. The Sri Sathya Sai Baba Centre of Shelton, Connecticut, held its first meeting on December 31st, 1982 at Mrs. Sunita Wadhwani and Dr. Ramesh Wadhwani's residence. 

In August of 1982, manifestations started happening all through the house (which was not a centre back then). These manifestations lasted for two weeks. Sathya Sai Baba came into a dream of Dr. Ramesh Wadhwani’s niece, who was visiting from West Africa and he told her, "Tell Ramesh that Sai Nivas will become a Sai Centre."

episodes.title: Sanathana Sai Sanjeevini (Portuguese)
episodes.description:
Sanathana Sai Sanjeevini (Portuguese)
00:00 Intro
00:12 Entrevista concedida à Rede Globo, do Rio Grande do Sul, a respeito do Sistema de Terapia Sanathana Sai Sanjeevinis... Curando com Orações.
02:30 Entrevista concedida a Rede RBS, em Porto Alegre, durante a realização do último Seminário Sanjeevini naquele estado da Federação.
14:42 Ashrams of India

https://www.saisanjeevini.org/

Ferramentas terapêuticas Sai Sanjeevini https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=IYV7f48tucg&ab_channel=MileneScotti

LINK PARA OS ARQUIVOS MENSIONADOS: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1cw2epjBehc1L35AQaelSjjqpF2uDDhmE

Chama-se Sanathana Sai Sanjeevii o Sistema de Terapia Complementar criado em 1995 por Poornima Nagpal, na Índia. A palavra “Sanathana” significa “Válido todo o tempo, atemporal”, a palavra “Sai” refere-se à Mãe Divina e “Sanjeevini” é o nome de uma planta oriunda do Himalaia capaz, segundo textos indianos, de curar qualquer enfermidade.

Todas as informações necessárias ao bom entendimento do Sistema e à sua aplicação estão reunidos no site brasileiro www.saisanjeevini.com.br. Nele o interessado pode ler diretamente ou baixar, para seu computador, o material necessário a tratar-se, sem qualquer custo. O Sistema se baseia em Orações, em Forma Gráfica. Existem, até o presente momento, 60 Gráficos ou Orações para Partes do Corpo e 186 Orações, em Forma Gráfica, para Enfermidades.

A Técnica consiste em, após receber do paciente o relato do que sente, relacionar as Orações das Partes do Corpo afetadas e os Eventos registrados. Isto feito o Usuário coloca um meio físico, por exemplo um copo de água, sobre cada Gráfico selecionado e faz uma prece dentro do seu Sistema de Crenças. Isto feito o paciente ingere esta água. Um exemplo corrente é o do paciente que tem uma dor de cabeça. No Sistema Sanjeevini, o paciente seleciona o Gráfico relacionado com a cabeça (Parte do Corpo) e aquele relacionado com a dor (Enfermidade ou Evento). Após a seleção basta colocar um copo mal cheio, de água, sobre um dos Gráficos, fazer uma Oração de sua predileção, movendo o copo para o Gráfico seguinte, e orando outra vez. O Sistema nasceu na Índia mas os pacientes são orientados a usar as Orações de sua denominação religiosa por serem aquelas que, naturalmente, tocam mais os seus corações.

Introduzido, no Brasil no ano de 1998, ganhou a Internet no ano de 2000, desde logo
possibilitando a independência dos buscadores em baixar o Manual do Usuário e começar a usar o Sistema sem qualquer custo ou ingerência.

Alfredo Augusto Cruz é seu divulgador, desde 1998, e o responsável pela tradução do original Inglês para o Português tanto do Manual do Usuário quanto do material existente no site. Alfredo Augusto é, ainda, moderador do Grupo Sanjeevini Brasil e do Grupo Sanjeevini internacional.

Todas estas atividades Alfredo Augusto exerce sob permissão da Fundação Sanjeevini, da Índia (www.saisanjeevini.org).

Desde o ano 2000 o Sistema Sanjeevini tem sido divulgado através de Seminários realizados em cidades Brasileiras. Além do território brasileiro, seu divulgador também apresentou um Seminário em Lisboa, Portugal e participou de outro na Áustria.

A cidade brasileira campeã na realização de Seminários é Porto Alegre com a marca de quatro realizados até hoje o que gerou forte repercussão e cobertura da Imprensa Televisiva (Rede Globo e RBS). Estima-se que o Rio Grande do Sul tenha o maior número de Usuários, hoje calculados em dez mil, segundo levantamento local.

About the Sai Sanjeevini Healing Prayers System;

Sanathana Sai Sanjeevini healing fragrances is a prayer based, self-learning spiritual healing system to awaken the inherent healing power and wisdom of every being. The healing system is offered with all humility, deep gratitude and selfless unconditional love for the wholistic well-being of the Lord’s entire creation in all the realms, irrespective of caste, colour, creed, language, gender, religion and nationality.

The book A Healer’s Guide on Sai Sanjeevini healing is a complete self-learning Guide. To know more about the Contents of A Healer’s Guide or to Browse or Download the Guide, please click on the link https://ashramsofindia.com/downloads/

episodes.title: Isaac Tigrett : The Secret Life Of Plants
episodes.description:
In 1973, Isaac was so amazed by a book called The Secret Life Of Plants that he bought the movie rights to this best-seller which explored paranormal phenomena. A few months later he sold the rights to Warner Bros. on the condition that he could accompany the scriptwriters for the forthcoming documentary around the world to observe the phenomena described in the book. While on the trip during breakfast one morning in a hotel dining-room in Aurangabad he heard a voice clearly saying, "You've come at last; I've been waiting for you." Turning round, he saw a picture on the wall of Sathya Sai Baba, whom he had never heard of and knew nothing about.

00:00 Intro from THE ISAAC TIGRETT TAPES https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=TEn8lcvTR-0&list=PLfvx-cbEOmxAuPeexroxX2zYiP98WzfUy&index=2&ab_channel=AshramsofIndia
02:27 Isaac Tigrett and the book THE SECRET LIFE OF PLANTS
10:25 An excerpt from the 1978 documentary THE SECRET LIFE OF PLANTS filmed in India ~ Jagadish Chandra Bose
14:10 The 1978 documentary THE SECRET LIFE OF PLANTS

Isaac Tigrett https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLfvx-cbEOmxAuPeexroxX2zYiP98WzfUy

The Secret Life of Plants: A Fascinating Account of the Physical, Emotional and Spiritual Relations Between Plants and Man. By Peter Tompkins, Christopher Bird.

Exploring the world of plants and its relation to mankind as revealed by the latest discoveries of scientists, The Secret Life of Plants includes remarkable information about plants as lie detectors and plants as ecological sentinels; it describes their ability to adapt to human wishes, their response to music, their curative powers, and their ability to communicate with man. Authors Peter Tompkins and Christopher Bird suggest that the most far-reaching revolution of the 20th century — one that could save or destroy the planet — may come from the bottom of your garden.

episodes.title: The Man of Miracles by Don C. Como (1975)
episodes.description:
The Man of Miracles by Don C. Como (1975).

Don C. Como Film https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLfvx-cbEOmxAxWqf_neI3tUNTQ0nkO95g

Hosted by Rod Serling. You are about to witness the apparently spontaneous manifestation of objects from thin air or, perhaps, another dimension.This particular phenomenon is only one among many attributed by his followers to a man of extraordinary mystical powers, an Indian mystic and guru known throughout the world as Sathya Sai Baba. Some of the psychic powers reported by his followers are the ability to read minds and see into the future, to cure disease by miraculous means, to project his astral body anywhere in the world and to adopt other forms, both human and animal. In this film you will witness events which may be unbelievable. However, try to keep an open mind, for some of this man's feats have been captured on film, and you will see them now as they actually happened.

Another Don C. Como film ~ Aliens From Spaceship Earth (1977) https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=cnFG-TBNKw0

episodes.title: Richard Bock Films : SATHYA SAI BABA IN KASHMIR : JUNE 1980
episodes.description:
SATHYA SAI BABA IN KASHMIR : JUNE 1980 is narrated and edited by Richard Bock.

Richard Bock was the Founder-President of Pacific Jazz and World Pacific Records. Through a long personal and professional relationship with Pandit Ravi Shankar, the world renowned sitar maestro, Mr. Bock was instrumental in introducing classical Indian music to large audiences in the West. He has produced several films on Sathya Sai Baba.

episodes.title: Sathya Sai Baba interview, 7th May 1989, Kodaikanal
episodes.description:
Sathya Sai Baba interview, 7th May 1989, Kodaikanal
00:00 The Gayatri Mantra is taught
06:03 A longer video of the interview (16 minutes)
22:21 Gayatri Mantra 

Sathya Sai Baba left Brindavan for Ooty accompanied by 27 Institute staff and students early in the morning of April 8th, 1989. Enroute, he stopped in Mysore for a short while before proceeding to Ooty. Baba stayed in Sai Shruti at Kodaikanal from the 11th of April to the 7th of May, 1989.

Fred Greenberg interviews Suri Bhagavantam, Howard Murphet, Don Heath, Sam Sandweiss, and Larry Smith in Prasanthi Nilayam, Christmas time in 1978. Video by Jack Lenchiner https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=NH2AQkffOFQ&t=89s&ab_channel=AshramsofIndia
