episodes.title: Louise Illig-Mooncie
episodes.description:
Two documentaries Louise filmed, ‘The Miracle of Puttaparthi’ in India and another in France ‘Chartres Cathedral – A Sacred Geometry,’ were inspired by Professor Keith Critchlow who was a British expert in Sacred Geometry. The docos were projects close to her heart and were made independently of her work for German TV.

This video is an edited version of two interviews with Louise first screened on https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL54U71PLpiD4HN4UigjPc1pzwA-gpdPvH

The Miracle of Puttaparthi (1994) https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ICeTx6j_ZzA&ab_channel=AshramsofIndia

Chartres Cathedral ~ A Sacred Geometry ~ Professor Keith Critchlow https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JEiBypewW48&ab_channel=AshramsofIndia

Sai Baba : God on Earth https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DuXsOJEaGQY&ab_channel=AshramsofIndia

2019 Interview with Louise Illig-Mooncie, author of ‘Encounter Between Two Worlds.' German-born Louise Illig Mooncie has written about the coming to terms with her country’s uneasy past. Her spiritual journey has taken her to the UK, India and Ireland and she shares her experiences in the book Encounter Between Two Worlds. 

Q This book feels like a lifetime’s journey.

It is indeed a life-time’s journey, my soul-journey. It has been a search for a deeper meaning and understanding of life’s challenges and mysteries. I have a wealth of memories from a very young age of my world in post-war Germany with all of its difficulties and secrets. That’s where it all started and it’s when I started to try and make sense of the past.

Obviously, Germany is my birthplace and I maintained strong links with it after I moved to the UK through working for German TV in London. Following my divorce and  raising my son as a single parent, I almost reached burn-out as I started my spiritual journey. I felt that by staying in England I would find what I was looking for.

This search for peace and understanding finally led me to a guru (teacher) in India. At the time, I wrote a diary about this life-changing journey and my slow spiritual transformation. I experienced a warm feeling of home-coming and a deep inner happiness in India. And indeed, this happiness is still in my heart. It is not tied to a particular country or place, it is there at any moment.

I must mention that Ireland also played a pivotal role in my life, having had a near-death experience while visiting a friend in Dublin (described in the book). I am very fond of the people and the landscape.

Q From your book, it seems that one of the key turning points was your meeting with The Jewish Ghost?

Without this extraordinary experience my book would not exist. This was the WOW effect in my life, changing my way of thinking about my own life, and everyone else’s for that matter, completely upside down. I started researching the possibility of reincarnation and felt very lucky when the BBC ran a well researched series of programmes about this subject, exactly when I needed it most (please see below for details).

For many years I did not speak to anyone about my ‘encounter’ with The Jewish Ghost, which I believed was a meeting with, or vision of, myself in a former life. I spoke about it only to the well known author and psychic from Tunbridge Wells, Peggy Mason. She shed some light on my experience and I describe this in my book.

Although my book is about my personal journey, I believe that the message of peace is relevant to all of us. We, as a human race, are all connected with one another. The whole of creation is connected, everything is one, one breath, one heartbeat. With my book I am trying to make an offering for peace in the world, peace in our hearts.

Q Does your belief in reincarnation affect the way that you live?

Definitely! I think about the consequences of my actions-reactions much more, and I’ve pledged to myself to live a truthful, loving and compassionate life.

Q Who or what has had the greatest influence on your life?

I’m a child of the 60s and people like Martin Luther King, Gandhi and Albert Schweizer (the humanitarian) made a great impact on me, not to forget the Beatles whose records my father banned me from playing at home. When I was young, my generation desperately wanted to change Germany, to make it less ‘dusty’ and rigid. In those days, we looked to England for guidance.

Buddhism, the Dalai Lama and my Indian Guru played a big role in my life. Now I’m a ‘free spirit’, believing that all of us have a place and a role to play: unity in diversity.

Chartres Cathedral 

Q I understand that before you wrote this book, you filmed a documentary in India called ‘The Miracle of Puttaparthi’ and another in France?

Yes, I made a documentary about an amazing hospital for the poor in the heart of rural India and another in France called ‘Chartres Cathedral – A Sacred Geometry.'

episodes.title: The Miracle of Puttaparthi (1994)
episodes.description:
This documentary is about the Super Specialty Hospital in Puttaparthi. On the 23rd of November 1990, Sathya Sai Baba announced the construction of a new hospital and that it would be designed and built in one year, and immediately upon completion the first operation would take place.

Another documentary is about the Super Specialty Hospital in Puttaparthi ~ The Hospital Video (1993) https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=SG8mgaGHoo4&ab_channel=AshramsofIndia

Louise Illig-Mooncie https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1cRhwHNSsNA&ab_channel=AshramsofIndia

Professor Keith Critchlow, Prasanthi Nilayam, 1995 https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Zf-XpYM-bJw&ab_channel=AshramsofIndia

As Sathya Sai Baba said, "this hospital was established with the noble aim of serving suffering humanity." It serves mainly the poor and is entirely free of cost.

The Sathya Sai Institute of Higher Medical Sciences opened on the 22nd of November 1991. It is located in Prasanthi Gram, close to the Sri Sathya Sai Airport, which is about 9km from the ashram (Prasanthi Nilayam) and Puttaparthi. The hospital provides free heart, kidney, and eye surgery. 

Isaac Tigrett, who founded the Hard Rock Cafe enterprise, secured Professor Keith Critchlow's expertise to design this hospital. His use of sacred geometry played a major role in many architectural designs and projects.

Isaac Tigrett Tapes https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=TEn8lcvTR-0&ab_channel=AshramsofIndia

Chartres Cathedral ~ A Sacred Geometry ~ Professor Keith Critchlow https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JEiBypewW48&ab_channel=AshramsofIndia

episodes.title: Chi è Sathya Sai Baba Preview
episodes.description:
Questa è una "Preview" di un  documento storico...il documentario di Victor Tognola girato a Puttaparthi nello Stato dell' Andhra Pradesh su Sathya Sai Baba, Avatar, Guru, il Cristo che camminò in India. Scienziati e uomini di cultura analizzano il fenomeno Sai Baba: Materializzava con le Sue mani la Cenere Sacra "Vibuthi," anelli, collane, orologi per i Suoi devoti. Gli innumerevoli Suoi miracoli, le guarigioni ed i fantastici racconti sull'Uomo Divino più straordinario che abbiate mai incontrato.

"Ho deciso di pubblicare questo piccolo montaggio come Testimonianza di Verità in quanto sono stato testimone personalmente di alcuni dei "Fenomeni Miracolosi," qui descritti, accaduti precisamente nel Gennaio del 1997 durante l'incontro in interview con Sathya Sai Baba e il Sottoscritto assieme ad un Gruppo di Devoti appartenenti al Centro Sai di Milano di Via Savona!" (Francesco Bernasconi).

Who is Sathya Sai Baba Preview. This is a "Preview" of a historical document...Victor Tognola's documentary filmed in Puttaparthi in Andhra Pradesh on Sathya Sai Baba, Avatar, Guru, the Christ who walked in India. Scientists and men of culture analyze the phenomenon Sai Baba: He materialised with His hands the Sacred Ash "Vibuthi," rings, necklaces, watches for His devotees. His countless miracles, healings and fantastic tales of the most extraordinary Divine Man you have ever met.

"I decided to publish this small montage as a Testimony of Truth as I personally witnessed some of the "Miraculous Phenomena," described here, which occurred precisely in January 1997 during the interview meeting with Sathya Sai Baba and the undersigned together with a Group of Devotees belonging to the Sai Center of Milan in Via Savona!" (Francesco Bernasconi).

episodes.title: Professor Kasturi talks to foreign devotees, Prasanthi Nilayam, 28th December, 1984
episodes.description:
00:00 Professor Kasturi talks to foreign devotees, Prasanthi Nilayam.
01:01:54 Professor Kasturi talks to foreign devotees, Prasanthi Nilayam, 28th December, 1984.

Narayana Kasturi (25 December 1897 – 14 August 1987) was an Indian writer, professor, and journalist.

Kasturi authored the work 'Sathyam Sivam Sundaram,' which was translated into English as 'Truth, Goodness, Beauty'. The first part of the book was published in 1960 and covers the first 34 years of the life of Sathya Sai Baba. Kasturi stated that he gathered information for the book from various sources, including Sathya Sai Baba's father and brother, as well as teachers from the schools he attended. The second part of the book was published in 1968, and the complete work was published around 1980. 

Narayana Kasturi was a writer and journalist who wrote for the magazine Koravanji in the 1940s and 1950s. He was known for discovering and promoting the work of young writers. Kasturi also wrote hagiographic accounts of Sathya Sai Baba's life and his own autobiography, entitled "Loving God: Eighty-five Years under the Watchful Eye of the Lord" was published in 1982 by Sri Sathya Sai Books and Publications.

episodes.title: The Hiranyagarbha Lingam
episodes.description:
The Hiranyagarbha Lingam was manifested by Sathya Sai Baba during a discourse to college students at Whitefield, near Bangalore, South India in 1993. Many people have been healed by touching this photo to their bodies. Sathya Sai Baba said that every home should have this picture as it has healing properties.

Download a copy of this photo https://ashramsofindia.com/photos/

episodes.title: Padre Pio (1968)
episodes.description:
This documentary was filmed and made by Mischa Scorer in 1968 the final year of Padre Pio's life.  Padre Pio who has since been canonised was venerated all over the world by hundreds of thousands who believed he worked miracles and bore on his body the five wounds of Christ - the Stigmata.

Padre Pio https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLfvx-cbEOmxBY0i8pz1AG76qWKgAHExRe

Pray, Hope and Don't Worry : A Celebration of Padre Pio (1987) https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=UbgXRODiT04&ab_channel=silencehugger

The Last Mass of St Pio of Pietrelcina https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qn1y1-nTouM&t=224s&ab_channel=uCatholic The Final Mass of St. Pio of Pietrelcina (Padre Pio) celebrated in the  Church of Santa Maria delle Grazie on September 22, 1968, the day before his death.

episodes.title: Sathya Sai Baba lighting the Hanukkah Menorah at Brindavan
episodes.description:
Sathya Sai Baba lighting the Menorah on the first day of Hanukkah in the Brindavan ashram in Whitefield, just outside of Bangalore, India, December, 1980

© Jack Lenchiner, 2019

The Hanukkah (Chanukah) Menorah is a nine-branched candelabrum lit during the eight-day holy Jewish festival of Hanukkah. On each night of Hanukkah, a new branch is lit. The ninth holder is for a candle used to light all other candles.

The menorah in this video belonged to Rubin Moses & Sons, a renowned shoe store on Commercial Street in Bangalore.

episodes.title: Al Druker (1986)
episodes.description:
Al Druker narrates a story about his youth in pre-war Germany.

David: In the talk that you gave yesterday, you related the story about meeting the SS colonel in the railway carriage, as you tried to escape from persecution in Nazi Germany. This impressive figure, dressed in the black uniform, must have absolutely taken your breath away, and yet he talked to you about the Bible and made that amazing statement, "There is no Moses to save you this time."

Al: I was just a kid of nine and, of course, I was terrified. I was frightened out of my wits when he came into that train compartment. I lived in Cologne and I was travelling across Germany to Poland and had stopped off in Berlin. In Berlin I had some well-to-do relations and they had decided to put me into this first class compartment, but unknown to me, Jews were not permitted to be there. I had the compartment to myself until the train stopped some twenty-five miles outside of Berlin and that was when this SS colonel appeared. So I sat there, petrified, expecting to be arrested, but he was charming He loosened his jacket and took off all of his imposing paraphernalia - the cap with the skull emblem on it, the black leather belt, the gun, the dagger, the leather gloves, the monocle and the big black leather boots.  He made himself comfortable and told me to sit comfortably and not to be afraid of him. So, in a sense, he became an ordinary guy for me. He talked to me about God and the Jews, quoting extensively from the Old Testament. He warned me that the Holocaust was coming and advised me to escape westwards not eastwards which, of course, is what I eventually did.

David: You said that you felt that the SS colonel was Sai Baba, that he manifested as that colonel to warn you, in fact, to save your life. Are you really sure of that?

Al: It is very clear to me now that the colonel was Swami. He simply didn't fit the SS character at all. There was no way that man would have taken the chance in Nazi Germany at that time of saying the things that he said to me, even if he felt that way.

David: The SS colonel came out with this amazing statement "There is no Moses to save you this time. You will have to be your own Messiah." What do you think that Swami meant when he said that? How do you view that message in the light of what eventually happened to the Jews? What would be the purpose of the Jews being subject to the Holocaust? Was it to prove to them that there is no Messiah?

Al: I wish I even had the beginning of some answers for that. I have no idea, David. But two world wars in two successive generations, which destroyed or uprooted hundreds of millions of people, and then the development of atomic and hydrogen weapons that promised to snuff out all of civilisation, and God knows what other insane weapons of death are in the offing to destroy Mankind, only proves what madness has come upon us in this Kali Yuga, and how absolutely vital it was in this time of darkness for the Avatar to come and rescue Mankind from itself. The genocide of the Jews is just an outward symptom of the genocidal feelings of hatred and mayhem inside all of us. Swami has come to correct that. As for the Messiah, I once had the chance to ask Swami whether he was the Messiah for which we had long been waiting. He answered "Not one Messiah. You are all Messiahs. You have the power to save yourself and to save others also." In other words, he will drive the chariot, he will direct us from within, but it is our job to save ourselves and we have been given the full power of God to transform the internal enemies of greed, hatred and jealousy, etc. which are polluting our hearts into the divine love that is Swami. For me, personally, Swami gave me the chance to clear up most of my haunting memories of Nazi Germany. It relates to the first time I had to leave India very unexpectedly.  Let me tell you the story.

Extract from an interview with Al Drucker http://saibaba.ws/articles/interviewaldrucker.htm

episodes.title: A Christian's Pilgrimage To The Master ~ Sathya Sai Baba by Lawrence Galante
episodes.description:
A Christian's Pilgrimage To The Master ~ Sathya Sai Baba by Lawrence Galante PhD https://www.holistic-arts.com/

Note: The booklet (A Christian's Pilgrimage to the Master) is not available to the public, as it was originally intended as an introduction to a much larger publication about Sathya Sai Baba.

00:00 Intro and interview with Michelle Hammerton. @saiforeveryone This is an excerpt from https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=eIGXydd5axw&t=561s&ab_channel=SaiforEveryone 
03:42 Sai Baba - Master & Avatar by Lawrence Galante. In 1976, Professor Lawrence Galante, had a dream to change his Master's Thesis in Comparative Religions from that on Saint Teresa of Avila, and instead to write his thesis on Sathya Sai Baba. Sceptical that the stories he had heard of Sai Baba's miracles might be made up by his devotees, and having suddenly and mysteriously received and unexpected $2,000. (enough for a plane trip to India). Dr Galante made a three month visit to Sai Baba's ashram to get the facts about this mysterious person first hand and for himself. This video was shot by L. Galante on a Super 8 camera. Most of the narrated dialogue, are direct quotes spoken and written by Sathya Sai Baba. @LawrenceGalante

Ashrams of India Volume 1 & 2 : Prasanthi Nilayam, in Puttaparthi, Andhra Pradesh (125 km NNE of Bangalore) is Sathya Sai Baba's main ashram. Brindavan, in Whitefield (on the outskirts of Bangalore) is the second ashram. Kodaikanal, a hill station in the south of India, is the third ashram.

Ashrams of India - ☆☆☆☆☆ - 5 out of 5 stars "Masterful - a well researched, serious look at many famous ashrams. Masterfully written and produced. So much detail and care has gone into this project which is a labour of love. We may only bow!"

Ashrams of India https://ashramsofindia.com/ "A massive 1749 page two volume book set that explores over 500 ashrams, temples, and other significant sites of eastern religious and spiritual interest located throughout the Indian sub-continent. The compilers have backed up their explorations with reems of classical, historical, and recent background information." - The Wanderling https://the-wanderling.com/

Ashrams of India is a travel guide that explores over 500 places of spiritual interest for the modern traveller.

Westerners had been visiting the ashrams of saints since the early part of the twentieth century. Their stories and the places that they visited, many of which are still in existence, are gathered together here in 'Ashrams of India' with practical advice including contact details, directions, and coordinates that link to Google Maps.

episodes.title: Richard Bock Films : AMRITHA ~ NECTAR OF IMMORTALITY ~ Guru Poornima Prasanti Nilayam 1970 (1984)
episodes.description:
Richard Bock joined Indra Devi in India for Baba’s forty-third birthday in November 1968, and returned with eight millimetre film and the recordings of Baba which became the LP entitled, Sathya Sai Baba Chants the Bhajans.

Richard Bock was the Founder-President of Pacific Jazz and World Pacific Records. Through a long personal and professional relationship with Pandit Ravi Shankar, the world renowned sitar maestro, Mr. Bock was instrumental in introducing classical Indian music to large audiences in the West. He has produced several films on Sathya Sai Baba.

00:00 Intro : Janet Bock appears in the 1975 documentary THE MAN OF MIRACLES. Ravi Shankar and George Harrison receive the darshan of Sathya Sai Baba in December 1976, at the Dharmakshetra Ashram in Bombay. This video is an excerpt from the film IN THE LIGHT OF PROPHECY by Richard and Janet Bock (who had earlier in 1972, introduced Ravi Shankar to Sathya Sai Baba).
01:36 Film

episodes.title: Richard Bock Films : Western devotees and visitors of Sathya Sai Baba talk of their experiences
episodes.description:
Richard Bock Films : Western devotees and visitors of Sathya Sai Baba talk of their experiences.

00:00 Intro
01:43 Walter and Elsie Cowan talk to Richard Bock
07:51 Indra Devi
08:50 Michele Malvin
10:18 Joel and Diana Riordan
13:06 Janet Bock
13:28 Samuel Sandweiss
14:16 Swami Kriyananda
15:52 Dr Jack Hislop

Richard Bock joined Indra Devi in India for Baba’s forty-third birthday in November 1968, and returned with eight millimetre film and the recordings of Baba which became the LP entitled, Sathya Sai Baba Chants the Bhajans.

Richard Bock was the Founder-President of Pacific Jazz and World Pacific Records. Through a long personal and professional relationship with Pandit Ravi Shankar, the world renowned sitar maestro, Mr. Bock was instrumental in introducing classical Indian music to large audiences in the West. He has produced several films on Sathya Sai Baba.

Ashrams of India is a travel guide that explores over 500 places of spiritual interest for the modern traveller.

Westerners had been visiting the ashrams of saints since the early part of the twentieth century. Their stories and the places that they visited, many of which are still in existence, are gathered together here in 'Ashrams of India' with practical advice including contact details, directions, and coordinates that link to Google Maps.

episodes.title: Richard Bock Films : THE LOST YEARS (1977)
episodes.description:
Richard Bock joined Indra Devi in India for Baba’s forty-third birthday in November 1968, and returned with eight millimetre film and the recordings of Baba which became the LP entitled, Sathya Sai Baba Chants the Bhajans.

00:00 Intro : Janet Bock appears in the 1975 documentary THE MAN OF MIRACLES. Ravi Shankar and George Harrison receive the darshan of Sathya Sai Baba in December 1976, at the Dharmakshetra Ashram in Bombay. This video is an excerpt from the film IN THE LIGHT OF PROPHECY by Richard and Janet Bock (who had earlier in 1972, introduced Ravi Shankar to Sathya Sai Baba).
01:36 Film

Dick and Janet Bock, whose painstaking research bore fruit in a film, The Lost Years (1977), and a book, The Jesus Mystery (1980). “It gradually dawned on us that those years were missing because someone had taken them out of the records, out of the Bible. We could not imagine Jesus would have appeared in Galilee at the age of 30 and hidden the major part of his life from his disciples whom he loved and asked to follow him.” 

“In examining historical records of the early Christian Church, it became evident that early Church councils, especially the First Council of Nicaea in 325 AD, changed many points of doctrine.”

episodes.title: Richard Bock Films : TRUTH IS MY NAME (1985)
episodes.description:
Richard Bock joined Indra Devi in India for Baba’s forty-third birthday in November 1968, and returned with eight millimetre film and the recordings of Baba which became the LP entitled, Sathya Sai Baba Chants the Bhajans.

Richard Bock was the Founder-President of Pacific Jazz and World Pacific Records. Through a long personal and professional relationship with Pandit Ravi Shankar, the world renowned sitar maestro, Mr. Bock was instrumental in introducing classical Indian music to large audiences in the West. He has produced several films on Sathya Sai Baba.

00:00 Intro : Janet Bock appears in the 1975 documentary THE MAN OF MIRACLES. Ravi Shankar and George Harrison receive the darshan of Sathya Sai Baba in December 1976, at the Dharmakshetra Ashram in Bombay. This video is an excerpt from the film IN THE LIGHT OF PROPHECY by Richard and Janet Bock (who had earlier in 1972, introduced Ravi Shankar to Sathya Sai Baba).
01:36 Film : Commentary by the revered biographer of Sri Sathya Sai Baba - Prof. N. Kasturi.

episodes.title: Fred Greenberg interviews five devotees of Sathya Sai Baba, Christmas 1978
episodes.description:
Fred Greenberg interviews Suri Bhagavantam, Howard Murphet, Don Heath, Sam Sandweiss, and Larry Smith in Prasanthi Nilayam, Christmas time in 1978. Video by Jack Lenchiner.

00:00 intro by Fred Greenburg
01:38 S. Bhagavantam

episodes.title: Brahmachari Atmananda Interview
episodes.description:
Brahmachari Atmananda : Blanca Schlamm  (1904-1985), an Austrian concert pianist, first travelled to India in 1925, to attend the 50th anniversary celebrations of The Theosophical Society, at its headquarters in Adyar, Madras. She returned to India in 1935, and remained there for the rest of her life. She went to Ramana Maharshi’s ashram in 1942 and stayed for six weeks. She taught at the Rajghat School in Benares and was a devotee of Anandamayi Ma.

episodes.title: The Wish Fulfilling Tree (Kalpavriksha) in Puttaparthi
episodes.description:
The Wish Fulfilling Tree (Kalpavriksha) in Puttaparthi. As a young boy, Sathya Sai Baba used to climb this hill by the Chitravathi River with his devotees to get to this old Tamarind Tree. He would often ask them, “What do you want?” Baba would then pick from the tree sugar candy, rosary, peppermint, or any of the fruit that they had requested. There is a cave here too that Baba would use at times, the entrance to which is next to the green concrete canopy.

episodes.title: Nadi Gruha, Bangalore
episodes.description:
Astrology professor, Sri Ganjur Narayana Shastry, traced a Shuka Naadi Grantha (a volume of palm leaf manuscripts by Sage Shuka) on Sathya Sai Baba. It consists of hundreds of palm leaf manuscripts in Sanskrit, thought to have been written over 5,000 yrs ago. It describes accurately and at great length, Baba's family tree and many other facts. Shastry thinks that the Shuka Naadi is a Chaitanya Grantha (a book of soul power) written by the ancient seers to form a bridge spanning time and space. After Ganjur Narayana Shastry's passing at age 40, his son is now a Sri Shuka Nadi Interpreter at 'Nadi Gruha,' Chamarajpet, Bangalore.

x Yugoslav doco (approx. 1986) ~ Tajanstvena Indija - Shuka Nadi - knjiga o buducnostii https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=vvBOottvVbw&t=0s&ab_channel=BataChale The host, Drago Plečko (Samobor, 04/04/1951), is a Chemist, promoter of alternative medicine and author of books on bioenergy, health and yoga. He is one of the pioneers in alternative fields - yoga, transcendental meditation, bioenergy, psychotronics and alternative spirituality. 

00:00 Intro
02:10 Drago Plečko nterviews Sri Ganjur Narayana Shastry (approx.1986), who was 40 at the time, he died shortly afterwards https://hr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Drago_Ple%C4%8Dko

episodes.title: The Wish Fulfilling Tree (Kalpavriksha) in Puttaparthi
episodes.description:
The Wish Fulfilling Tree (Kalpavriksha) in Puttaparthi. As a young boy, Sathya Sai Baba used to climb this hill by the Chitravathi River with his devotees to get to this old Tamarind Tree. He would often ask them, “What do you want?” Baba would then pick from the tree sugar candy, rosary, peppermint, or any of the fruit that they had requested. There is a cave here too that Baba would use at times, the entrance to which is next to the green concrete canopy.

episodes.title: Sathya Sai Baba : Pilgrimage to Baba (1993)
episodes.description:
Ashrams of India Volume 1 & 2 : Prasanthi Nilayam, in Puttaparthi, Andhra Pradesh (125 kilometres NNE of Bangalore) is Sathya Sai Baba's main ashram. Brindavan, in Whitefield (on the outskirts of Bangalore) is the second ashram. Kodaikanal, a hill station in the south of India, is the third ashram.

episodes.title: Lingua Italiana ~ Don Mario Mazzoleni interview, Puttaparthi
episodes.description:
Interview with Don Mario Mazzoleni, Puttaparthi

A Catholic Priest Meets Sai Baba https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=vERgnkzlXsk&ab_channel=AshramsofIndia

GIANCARLO ROSATI: DON MARIO MAZZOLENI PARLA DI SAI BABA https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=bllsTBkrMbk&ab_channel=SriSathyaSaiGlobalCouncilItalia-Zone6

SATHYA SAI BABA SU ANTENNA TRE con G.ROSATI E DON MAZZOLENI (1° parte) https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Ss6FUdXXKEM&ab_channel=SriSathyaSaiGlobalCouncilItalia-Zone6

A CATHOLIC PRIEST MEETS SAI BABA (1991) In the course of his spiritual inquiry, a Catholic priest meets an enlightening and extraordinary being: Sathya Sai Baba, a spiritual Master who lives in Puttaparthi, India. Like Jesus, Sai Baba performs miracles, materialises objects, heals the sick, and knows the past, present and future of everything that exists. Above all a transcendent peace and harmony flow from Him.

Who is Sai Baba? A saint, a magician, a demon, God in person? Don Mario Mazzoleni discovers how Sai Baba's teachings mirror those of Jesus Christ. Telling the story of his encounter with Sai Baba, and describing life in the ashram, he narrates the progress of his own spiritual search. His path begins with the need to detach himself from dogma and prejudice, and leads to the discovery of the fundamental unity of all religious creeds, a unity which transcends all cultural differences.

Don Mario Mazzoleni is 48 years old. He has a degree in Moral Theology from the Higher Institute of Theological Sciences of the Alphonsian Academy in Rome. In the 1970s he was associated with the Vatican Radio and the newspaper L'Avvenire. He was particularly concerned with the relationship of the mass media to spiritual instruction. He has been studying the miracle that is Sai Baba for twelve years, and has made eight trips to His ashram.

Mario Mazzoleni l' intervista

L'autore, un giornalista del Vaticano che si reca in India per conoscere Sai Baba redige una sorta di reportage espresso in forma autobiografica. Durante i suoi viaggi, egli cerca di intervistare Sai Baba, prima con di- staccato scetticismo, poi, man mano, con sempre maggiore partecipa-
zione.

Sono nove le interviste che il "reporter vaticanense”, ancora sacerdote, ha con Sai Baba, al quale sottopone le domande più svariate e scottanti: meriti e demeriti della religione, la morte, il dolore, la reincarnazione, i miracoli e i giochi "divini" di Sai Baba, l'influsso del pensiero, la medianicità, l'autoguarigione, la sessualità, il matrimonio, ecc.

In un capitolo strettamente autobiografico e storico sono riportati i dia- loghi dell'autore con i superiori, vescovi e cardinali, la sua sincera risposta alle minacce di scomunica e i decreti testuali del provvedimento mai pubblicati prima d'ora: una vera anteprima.

Mario Mazzoleni, 54 anni, fu ordinato sacerdote nel Giugno 1969, e perfezionò gli studi teologici a Roma negli anni 1972-75, conseguendo la Licenza in Scienze Teologiche e il Dottorato in Sacra Teologia Morale. Dal 1976 al 1979 colla- borò con il quotidiano L'Avvenire e con Radio Vaticana. Studioso e cultore di filosofia, ha compiuto alcuni studi personali e viaggi in terra indiana, visitando vari ashram e, fra tutti i Saggi incontrati sia personalmente che mediante lo studio del loro insegnamento (Babaji Haidakhan, Krishnamurti, Yogananda, Kriyananda, Maharishi Mahesh Yogi, ecc.), trova in Sai Baba una grandezza spirituale mai riscontrata in altri. È autore di alcuni libri, il più noto dei quali - "Un sacerdote incontra Sai Baba" - ha avuto una larga diffusione ed è stato finora tradotto in dieci lingue.

episodes.title: Connie Shaw : Who is Sai Baba ? (1990)
episodes.description:
Connie Shaw speaks at the Global Sciences Conference in Denver, Colorado, August 1990. Connie and her fiancé Jim Wright first visited Sathya Sai Baba's ashram, Prasanthi Nilayam, on the 18th of March, 1982. She is the author of Wake Up Laughing: My Miraculous Life With Sai Baba (2000). episodes.title: Sathya Sai Baba (November, 1975)
episodes.description:
Sathya Sai Baba | 50th Birthday Celebrations - A Documentary.

The 50th birthday celebrations (23rd November, 1975) of Sathya Sai Baba became a 10 day (14th - 24th November) Brahmotsavam (a festival of Divine bliss and ecstasy).

In this 1976 film, we see the first Rathotsavam and the inauguration of the Sarva Dharma Aikya Stupa, interviews by devotees such as Dr. S Bhagavantham and Dr. John Hislop. 

Sathya Sai Baba arrives in a helicopter on the morning of His birthday, officiating the 3rd World Conference of SSSIO, presiding over the Purushottama Yagna, inaugurating the Sarva Dharma Stupa, and swinging on the Jhoola on the final evening.

episodes.title: A Taxi Ride in India
episodes.description:
00:00 The story is first narrated in the video by Jack Hislop on the 27th of May 1989, in Eureka, Missouri, USA. 
03:15 In the second part, Heidi King recalls the events. 
05:03 Heidi recalls the event in 2019.

In the taxi were the driver, Jack and Victoria Hislop, Heidi King and her parents. Dr Jack Hislop writes - One evening in 1973, we left Brindavan at about 8 pm to return to Bangalore. There were five of us in the taxi, plus the driver. The Bangalore road is only two lanes. Some miles from Brindavan, we overtook a bus. Although the driver of our taxi saw some lights ahead, he estimated that there was plenty of time to pass the bus. He could not have been more mistaken – the lights ahead were that of a car moving at a very high speed towards us.

In this particular area, the layout of the road was also deadly. The road was under repair, and there was a high bank of dirt and rocks covering the side of the road. Thus, there was no possibility of the oncoming car being the miraculous escape able to swerve off the road. We, too, could not take evasive action, for the bus was on our left and the road-repair material on the right, and by this time the oncoming car was directly in front of us. A foolish driver in that car and an equally foolish driver in our taxi! …. The lights of the oncoming car now struck directly into the windscreen of our taxi. The cars could not have been more than a second or so apart. We were stunned. Not one of us remembered Baba or called out to Him. We felt we were as good as dead, and we instinctively tensed for the crash. But at that very moment something happened that was without any rational explanation. At one moment the two cars were upon each other, about to be smashed in a fatal head-on collision. The very next moment, the oncoming car was behind us, and we were continuing to pass the bus with a clear road ahead. Looking back, we could see the red tail-lamp of the other car. There was no crash.

The next day we drove out to Brindavan as usual at 8 am so as to be waiting near the veranda for Baba to appear for morning Darshan. As soon as He came into the room, I touched the Lotus feet and said, “We want to thank Baba for saving our lives last night.”

Baba smiled and said, “Yes, that was a close one. You were so shocked that not even one of you called out for Swami! But Swami saved you anyway.” Then He turned to a group of men and in Telugu told them the entire story of the incident.

I then said, “Swami, You must have altered time and space in order to save us.” Baba just smiled and did not answer.

episodes.title: Prasanthi Nilayam (1989)
episodes.description:
Trip to India, tour of Sai Baba's Ashram and its surroundings. By Anne Rodriguez Barfoot-Argentina, filmed by Renzo Zanutel and Martina de Col-Italy-edited by SAI BABA TV.

00:00 Prasanthi Nilayam, 1989
39:55 Darshan, 1989
01:29:10 Sathya Sai Baba’s place of birth in Puttaparthi, 1989
01:35:33 Puttaparthi, 1989
01:43:50 The Wish Fulfilling Tree in Puttaparthi, 1989
01:46:16 Sathya Sai Baba devotees Fred and Vallery, 1989

1989 was  a year of great movement in my life. I went on vacation to San Marcos Sierras with my boyfriend Pablo. In the middle of one night, we joined in with the Mahashivaratri singing for with a group of Sai Baba Devotees who were in the field next to the campsite. The following morning we joined a Human Values workshop, based on the story by Enrique Barrios, Ami el Niño de las Estrellas.

The year passed, after various situations and transformations in my life, in September of that 1989, the possibility of traveling to India arose. Actually, I first thought about traveling to Spain, Holland, Denmark, but I woke up one morning knowing that I had to go to India, they asked me why and I answered that I only knew that I was going, so I did all the paperwork to travel, and I left everything, my job, my house, my eldest son was living with his father in Villa Mercedes San Luis, a month later on October 1, I was on the plane traveling to India, to Sai Baba's Ashram.

Arriving alone, I met an Italian couple Renzo Zanutel and Martina De Col, we filmed ashram to share with people, friends, family, or to screen it on TV some day.

Anne Rodriguez Barfoot.

episodes.title: SOUL OF THE EAST part 4 - Ashrams, places of peace and knowledge
episodes.description:
SOUL OF THE EAST part 4 - Ashrams, places of peace and knowledge / DUŠE VÝCHODU 4 - Ášramy, místa klidu a poznání / 1992 Czech documentary. The Soul of the East is a seven-part documentary series focused on various manifestations of spiritual life in today's India. Created in Studio 61 T. Formáčka KF a.s. Viliam Poltikovič - author and director of the entire project. Filming took place from the beginning of December 1991 to the end of February 1992.

People in India have the opportunity to become familiar with spiritual knowledge, the essence of yoga, in ashrams and yoga centers. In the ashram of Swami Gitananda in the south of India, we see morning meditation on the shore with the many foreigners who have come here to gain energy and spiritual instruction. 

Nearby is Auroville - the lotus city of the future, which was built and is being built by people from 40 countries of the world. The impetus for its construction was the teachings of the great thinker Aurobindo imbued with truth, love and knowledge.

The personality of Ramana Maharishi, the sage of Mount Arunachala, is well known to the spiritually minded intellectuals of the Western world. Today he is no longer alive, but foreigners from all over the world still come to his ashram. 

In the great ashram at Mont Abu we find the World Spiritual University of the Brahma Kumaris movement. They are concerned not only with the inner knowledge of our being and essence, but also with maintaining and spreading peace in the world.

The beautiful large ashram has Sathya Sai Baba, who is considered a divine incarnation and is undoubtedly the most famous spiritual figure in contemporary India. He is worshiped not only in India but also all over the world where he has 6000 centers. At least 20,000 people from all over the world come to greet him every day. The atmosphere of the ashram is remarkable, people are clearly changing for the better. In addition to teachings and miracles, Sai Baba implements an extensive social program - he builds schools, hospitals, scientific workplaces all over India - everything is accessible to everyone and free of charge.

Comprehensive teaching of yoga and spiritual teachings is in famous ashrams in Rishikesh such as Shivananda and Parmarth Niketan.

episodes.title: Scottish-born Indian travel writer and mountain lover, Bill Aitken
episodes.description:
William McKay Aitken is a Scottish-born Indian travel writer and mountain lover. He is the author of a number of books about India, its mountains, rivers and its steam trains.

Bill Aitken's story can be found in this article https://ashramsofindia.com/early-western-devotees-of-sathya-sai-baba-from-the-1970s-to-the-1980s/

00:00 To the source of the Ganga https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=XIkNxa97OEA&ab_channel=AshramsofIndia

episodes.description:
The full 2012 interview with Derek can be seen here https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=KBOhiraBnEE&ab_channel=SriSathyaSaiOfficial

Derek travels to the Brindavan Ashram of Sathya Sai Baba in March, 1999.

Ashrams of India Volume 1 & 2 : Prasanthi Nilayam, in Puttaparthi, Andra Pradesh (125 kilometres NNE of Bangalore) is Sathya Sai Baba's main ashram. Brindavan, in Whitefield (on the outskirts of Bangalore) is the second ashram. Kodaikanal, a hill station in the south of India, is the third ashram.

episodes.title: Bob Raymer
episodes.description:
Bob Raymer (1921 - 2008) was a former commercial airline pilot who met Paramahansa Yogananda in the latter years of Yogananda's life. Yogananda initiated Raymer into Kriya Yoga and made him a minister of SRF. Raymer conducted services in Minneapolis for many years, telephoning Yogananda before each to receive his blessing. Raymer later moved to Hawaii where he conducted his Clear Light retreats, and then to Song of the Morning retreat center in Michigan, where he was the spiritual director from 1991 to 2004.

00:00 Bob meets Yogananda for the first time
00:32 Photos - Soundtrack : Sarah Jarosz - Orange and Blue https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=zElwvwrmjJI&ab_channel=SarahJarosz
01:47 Bob talks about Paramahansa Yogananda, Babaji, Dr Banerjee (the nephew of Tiger Swami), Sathya Sai Baba and Santosh Dada
05:49 Bob Raymer meets Paramahansa Yogananda
14:35 Bob Raymer at Ananda Village, 1993
32:54 Sam Gabby recalls Bob Raymer
35:45 Bob Raymer talks about George Gaye

PILOT BABA ASHRAM, former ashram of Soham Swami (aka Tiger Swami), where he attained mahasamadhi. His body was afterwards taken to the Palitpur Ashram of his guru, Tibbetibaba. 
PALITPUR ASHRAM of Tibbati Baba/Pragya Mandir, one of Tibbati Baba’s two ashrams and the samadhi of Tiger Swami.

episodes.title: ASHRAMS by Arnaud Desjardins
episodes.description:
ASHRAMS by Arnaud Desjardins

Arnaud Desjardins Films https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLfvx-cbEOmxB0712LqWHeQfKAmRlCiBqC

00:00 Photos
01:42 ASHRAMS 
36:42 Entrevue avec Arnaud Desjardins en 1972 
01:04:38 Sagesses bouddhistes - Rencontre avec Arnaud Desjardins, un parcours d'exception
01:20:35 Ashrams of India

Arnaud Desjardins – Excerpt from Enlightenment Magazine https://ashramsofindia.com/arnaud-desjardins-excerpt-from-enlightenment-magazine/

Arnaud Desjardins (1925 - 2011) was a French author. He was a producer at the Office de Radiodiffusion Télévision Française from 1952 to 1974, and was one of the first high-profile practitioners of Eastern religion in France.

He worked on television documentaries about spiritual traditions not well known to many Europeans at the time, including Hinduism, Tibetan Buddhism, Zen, and Sufism from Afghanistan.

He was the son of Jacques Guérin-Desjardins. He was part of the George Gurdjieff group, his first contact with mysticism. Educated in a Protestant Christian environment, he was exposed to spiritual aspects of Christianity on a visit to a trappist Catholic monastery. He then became interested in yoga, and when asked to direct a film for French television, he chose to make a series of films on India, for which he gained attention for his first film, Ashrams.

In 1959 he travelled to India, where he met and filmed Swami SHIVANANDA SARASWATI of Rishikesh, Swami Ramdas, and Anandamayi Ma. Back in France, his finished documentary was shown on national television and very well received by the public as well as critics. He then specialized in documentaries on living spiritual traditions, filming with his 16-millimeter camera, assisted only by his wife. They traveled for extensive periods, taking their two children with them. In 1965, Desjardins produced several documentaries on the Tibetans, became close to the Dalaï Lama, and went on to produce films on Zen Buddhism in Japan and Sufi brotherhoods in Afghanistan. Those films, available today on DVD and videotapes, are considered unique documents. The expeditions enabled Desjardins to spend a lot of time in close contact with some of the greatest teachers of the East and to pursue his spiritual quest while developing a growing reputation in France as a filmmaker and lecturer.

He became a practitioner of Adhyatma yoga, which is a branch of Advaita Vedanta.

https://hinduism.en-academic.com/228/Desjardins%2C_Arnaud

episodes.title: Srirangapatna Orphanage
episodes.description:
Ashrams of India Volume 1 : Srirangapatna Orphanage : The miracles of amritha and vibuthi manifesting at this orphanage in Mysore.

00:00 The Temple at the orphanage in 1990, excerpt from https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=CFZoK7dKqQo&ab_channel=AshramsofIndia
01:17 The Temple in 2004
03:43 James Redmond and Rick Raines visit the Temple during the 1990s
07:20 A visit to the Temple in 2022
08:11 Rough footage taken inside the Temple
10:31 Ashrams of India

Mr Halagappa (d. 2019) devoted his reformed life to running this orphanage. In his younger days, he was a baggage lifter (a thief) until Sathya Sai Baba told him to stop and open the Sri Ranga Patna orphanage. He bought land on the banks of the river Cauvery and started the orphanage with four children in 1965. Halagappa says that in 1965 Sai Baba visited the ashram to see and guide the activities here while on his way to Kodaikanal. Sathya Sai Baba continued to regularly stop at this ashram to speak and give darshan to Halagappa and his family members until 1985.

A small temple or shrine is part of the orphanage, it contains many photographs of Sai Baba covered with materialized vibhuti. The temple also has in its possession some amulets (with portraits of Sathya Sai Baba and Shirdi Sai Baba) which were blessed by Sathya Sai Baba more than 30 years ago. When the amulets are held, amritha continuously flows from them. Amritha is sacred honey nectar or ambrosia that is also known as amrita and amrit, it is Sathya Sai Baba’s holy substance that has a distinctive taste and odour. The amulets are for all to hold and see.

There is also carved in black stone on a pedestal, feet representing those of Shirdi Baba’s which exude a particularly fragrant amrit. These symbolic feet were installed there on Sai Baba’s directions by N. Kasturi in the 1960s. 

Amrit is the ‘nectar of the gods,’ which in the ancient Ramayana gave eternal life to those who were fortunate enough to taste it. Those who did not succeed were consigned to more lifetimes as demonic asuras. It is also considered to be symbolic of the subtle liquid secreted by the pituitary gland of yogic adepts which is said to confer immortality.

episodes.title: Padre Pio : Veneration and Confession
episodes.description:
Padre Pio : Veneration and Confession, scenes from the movie Padre Pio Miracle Man https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nKM909nHz4A&ab_channel=SacredArtbeads : https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=0619i9brA0M&ab_channel=Mrs.ThereseMastersStacy

00:04 Scene 1 Padre Pio arranges for the purchase of a shawl for one of the parishioners 
02:02 Scene 2 Padre Pio delivers bread to a poor family
03:02 Scene 3 Cleonice
05:27 Scene 4 Padre Pio prevents Cleonice’s rape
06:49 Scene 5 Padre Pio protects soldiers
08:39 Scene 6 Candeloro
10:08 Scene 7 Padre Pio recognises the young priest Karol Józef Wojtyła as the future Pope (Pope John Paul II)
11:44 Scene 8 Padre Pio gives his father the Last Rites
15:56 Scene 9 Lea Padovani
19:35 Scene 10 Pope Paul VI requests the prayers of Padre Pio

Padre Pio was born Francesco Forgione on May 25, 1887 in Pietrelcino, Italy. Raised in a pious Catholic family, Francesco entered the friary in 1903 and one year later received the Capuchin habit, taking the name Pio. He was ordained a priest and transferred to several sites until 1916, when he arrived in San Giovanni Rotondo, where he remained for the last 52 years of his life.

Padre Pio is admired for his fervent love of Christ and the Virgin Mary and is known as the great mystic of modern times.

He experienced a wide variety of supernatural abilities and miraculous events including: the reading of souls; prophecy; bilocation (being in two places at once); the odour of sanctity; discernment of spirits; living on very little sleep; miraculous healings; personal visits from Jesus and Mary; and daily communication with his guardian angel.

Padre Pio's most famous spiritual gift is the stigmata, which he received in 1918 while praying before a crucifix. He is said to have bled from the five wounds of Christ for the rest of his life, which caused him great suffering and embarrassment.

Because of Padre Pio's great holiness and gifts, the devil is said to have waged war on the friar throughout his life, which included physical attacks resulting in cuts, bruises and other visible marks.

Padre Pio was devoted to all those who sought his help, but he was especially devoted to the souls in purgatory. He once said, "More souls of the dead from purgatory, than of the living, climb this mountain to attend my Masses and seek my prayers."

In 1940, Padre Pio began plans to open a hospital in San Giovanni Rotondo, to be named the Casa Sollievo della Sofferenza or Home for the Relief of Suffering. The hospital opened in 1956, and is considered one of the most efficient hospitals in Europe.

In 1956, construction began on a new church of Santa Maria delle Grazie to accommodate the many pilgrims who came to visit Padre Pio. Designed by Giuseppe Gentile Boiano, the church was consecrated by the Bishop of Foggia in 1959. This church remains the central focus of the sanctuary today.

In 1962, Bishop Karol Wojtyła, later Pope John Paul II, wrote to Padre Pio to ask him to pray to God for Dr. Wanda Poltawska, a friend in Poland who was suffering from cancer. Later, Dr. Poltawska's cancer was found to have regressed; medical professionals were unable to offer an explanation for the regression. It is also rumoured that during this time that Padre Pio had predicted Wojtyła would become Pope.

episodes.title: Ganeshpuri Days : Memoirs of a Western Yogi by Swami Shankarananda
episodes.description:
An interview with the author, Swami Shankarananda.

About the book;
In 1970, driven by a search for purpose and meaning, a young New Yorker leaves his promising academic career to travel to India seeking yogic wisdom. After many adventures, he arrives at the feet of the great Siddha master Baba Muktananda, in the holy village of Ganeshpuri. Here, he experiences the awakening of the kundalini energy.

With enthusiasm, sincerity and candid self-reflection, Swami Shankarananda depicts his profound relationship with his Guru and the inner voyage of his transformation. He takes the reader on a mystical journey in which he does battle with his ego and his own negative tendencies and connects with the inner divine energy. Under Baba's guidance he emerges from a twelve-year apprenticeship as a knower of the Self and a Guru in his own right.

'Ganeshpuri Days' is a beautifully written account that will inspire readers to awaken to the true Self and fulfil their highest potential

About the interview;
'By 1970, the great master of kundalini awakening Baba Muktananda had built a mighty school of transformation at his ashram in Ganeshpuri, fifty miles from Bombay. Over the next few years, a small group of highly dedicated men and women from India and the West gathered there, eager to live under Muktananda’s powerful but enlightened discipline in order to realise the inner Self. Later, many thousands would flood the ashram, but what was it really like in those austere and intense early days?

Swami Shankarananda is one of Baba Muktananda’s senior-most initiates. In this remarkably candid account, he brings the ashram of those years to vivid life. An intimate portrait of Muktananda emerges as well as a riveting depiction of Shankarananda’s own inner process.'

In this interview Swamiji speaks openly and with detail about his time in India, as well as the process of writing the book.

You can buy a copy of the book from the website, www.ganeshpuridays.com

Ashrams of India Volume 1
*Gurudev Siddha Peeth, the ashram of Swami Muktananda
*Swami Nityananda Ashram, the main ashram of Bhagawan Nityananda. Across the road from Kailash Niwas is the Shaligram Mandir. Sri Shaligram Swami (1929-1961) lived in Ganeshpuri for many years and was one of the chief disciples of Nityananda.
*Nityananda Ashram, the ashram of Nityananda in Kanhangad, where he is thought to have stayed for 25 years
*Shanti Mandir Ashram, the Haridwar ashram of Nityananda (brother of Gurumayi Chidvilasananda)

episodes.title: Muktananda : La Ricerca Della Felicità
episodes.description:
Documentario girato negli anni ’70 dell’Istituto Luce, per l’efficacia della meditazione Siddha insegnata da Baba Muktananda per conseguire la vera felicità. Negli anni ’70 Swami Muktananda portò in Occidente l’antica tradizione spirituale del lignaggio del suo maestro. Diede l’Iniziazione shaktipat, fino ad allora poco conosciuta, a migliaia di cercatori spirituali. Creò in India la fondazione Gurudev Siddha Peeth, per amministrare il lavoro svolto nel paese, e negli Stati Uniti creò la SYDA Foundation per amministrare le attività della meditazione Siddha Yoga nel mondo.

Documentary filmed in the 70s by Istituto Luce, about the effectiveness of the Siddha meditation taught by Baba Muktananda to achieve true happiness. In the 1970s Swami Muktananda brought the ancient spiritual tradition of his master's lineage to the West. He gave the hitherto little-known Shaktipat Initiation to thousands of spiritual seekers. He created the Gurudev Siddha Peeth Foundation in India to administer the work done in the country, and in the United States he created the SYDA Foundation to administer the Siddha Yoga meditation activities around the world.

Ashrams of India Volume 1
*Gurudev Siddha Peeth, the ashram of Swami Muktananda
*Swami Nityananda Ashram, the main ashram of Bhagawan Nityananda. Across the road from Kailash Niwas is the Shaligram Mandir. Sri Shaligram Swami (1929-1961) lived in Ganeshpuri for many years and was one of the chief disciples of Nityananda.
*Nityananda Ashram, the ashram of Nityananda in Kanhangad, where he is thought to have stayed for 25 years
*Shanti Mandir Ashram, the Haridwar ashram of Nityananda (brother of Gurumayi Chidvilasananda)

episodes.title: Patal Bhuvaneshwar Cave
episodes.description:
Ashrams of India Volume 2 : Patal Bhuvaneshwar Cave : Cave that is said to be the dwelling place of Shiva and his consort Pavarti. Pilgrims today recount visionary experiences, including seeing great light beings.

This is an abridged version of The Secret Cave in India : Patal Bhuvaneshwar https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Z0xse3UObfc&ab_channel=AshramsofIndia

Patal Bhuvaneshwar, literally means the subterranean shrine of Lord Shiva. It is a limestone cave temple at 1350 m, 14 km from Gangolihat in the Pithoragarh district of Uttarakhand. It is located 500 m from the village of Bhuvneshwar. It is believed that Shankaracharya also visited this place in 1191 AD. There is an inscription on the entrance, dating back to the 14th century AD. This underground cave enshrines Lord Shiva and 33 crore Gods (330 million), one theory is that the number of 330 million gods refers to the total count of the then known population of all the humans and living beings that ever walked the Earth, including the 8.4 million jeeva rasi (living species) signifying that god exists in every living being.

episodes.title: Richard Lannoy's photographs of Anandamayi Ma
episodes.description:
Richard Lannoy presents and discusses some of his photographs of Anandamayi Ma.

I first met Anandamayi in 1954, on my first visit to Varanasi. I was a freelance photographer specialising in magazine reportage. I was always on the lookout for fresh feature material.

I was 26 travelling rough, and hard pressed to locate marketable themes in a notoriously competitive field. I heard of Anandamayi through a remarkable Austrian pianist and schoolteacher, Blanca Schlamm, who had been a follower of Krishnamurti for 30 years. On the staff of a school inspired by the ideas of Krishnamurti, she was then in the process of readjusting her whole life to the new perspective opened up for her by Anandamayi. She already had considerable experience of Mataji's teaching and devoted scrupulous care to the translation of her words into English for the ashram magazine, under the name Atmananda. Moreover, due to her linguistic abilities Atmananda was frequently called upon to act as interpreter, not only for foreigners in private talks with Mataji, but also for many Indians who understood neither Bengali nor Hindi, the two languages in which Mataji conversed.

episodes.title: Prema Sai Baba and the story of Srirangapatna
episodes.description:
Past narratives suggest Srirangapatna as one of the places considered as a likely place of birth for Prema Sai Baba.

episodes.title: Prasanthi Nilayam in 1989
episodes.description:
Ashrams of India Volume 1 : Prasanthi Nilayam in 1989. This film is an excerpt from 'The Way To Baba: A Pilgrimage to Prasanthi Nilayam' by Elda Hartley https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gew7DsGBrbw&ab_channel=AshramsofIndia The cover photo is of Elda Voelkel Hartley during her acting career in the 1930s.

The documentary from 1990, was filmed in 1989, and was written, directed, and produced by Elda Hartley of the Hartley Foundation. It shows life in Sai Baba's ashram and talks about his teachings. Michael Oliver (USA), Hans Schaefer (Germany), Irmgard (Germany), Steve Angel (USA), Christiane Ansmeier (Germany), Vernon Sylvester (USA), Howard Murphet (Australia), Iris Murphet (Australia), Ellen Young (USA), Al Drucker (USA), Art Drumheller (USA), and Gail Ruiz (Mexico) talk about their experience with and views on Sai Baba and his teachings. One interviewee identified as Heidi Schaefer is actually Irmgard, a long time devotee who stayed for many years.

The soundtrack is 'Sri Ma' (dedicated to Anandamayi Ma) by Chloe Goodchild https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=bwSAbVhiuaI&ab_channel=NakedVoice This track is available on Chloe's album 'Fierce Wisdom.' Chloe says of Anandamayi Ma "One of the greatest Indian woman saints of the twentieth century. Her presence and unchanging Love has sustained, inspired and shaped my understanding."

episodes.title: Dasara celebrated in Prasanthi Nilayam in 1972
episodes.description:
Dasara celebrated in Prasanthi Nilayam in 1972. Vijayadashami (Sanskrit: विजयदशमी, romanized: Vijayadaśamī), also known as Dussehra, Dasara or Dashain, is a major Hindu festival celebrated at the end of Navaratri every year. It is observed on the tenth day in the Hindu calendar month of Ashvin, the seventh month of the Hindu Luni-Solar Calendar, which typically falls in the Gregorian months of September and October.

All genuine gurus do the unexpected, to shock and transform people: Neem Kaloli Baba, Nityananda… Even the ‘gentle’ sage of Arunachala, Ramana Maharshi, set up ashram builder Annamalai Swami to be in conflict with the ashram administrative head and told him not to say that he, Ramana, had told him to go ahead with a building project against the official administration’s plan.

episodes.title: The Way To Baba: A Pilgrimage to Prasanthi Nilayam by Elda Hartley
episodes.description:
Documentary from 1990, filmed in 1989, was written, directed, and produced by Elda Hartley of the Hartley Foundation. It shows life in Sai Baba's ashram and talks about his teachings. Michael Oliver (USA), Hans Schaefer (Germany), Irmgard (Germany), Steve Angel (USA), Christiane Ansmeier (Germany), Vernon Sylvester (USA), Howard Murphet (Australia), Iris Murphet (Australia), Ellen Young (USA), Al Drucker (USA), Art Drumheller (USA), and Gail Ruiz (Mexico) talk about their experience with and views on Sai Baba and his teachings. One interviewee identified as Heidi Schaefer is actually Irmgard, a long time devotee who stayed for many years.

Hinduism and The Song of God (1978) https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=KL04TK6KPvQ&ab_channel=AshramsofIndia

Elder Hartley Films https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=307q27leEq8&list=PLfvx-cbEOmxAByIDUroF0Ie91XO-an0tL&ab_channel=AshramsofIndia

Hartley Films was started in 1971 by founder Elda E. Hartley. Elda and Irving Hartley made newsreels and travel films from the 1930s through the 1960s. Some of the images that now illustrate American history were shot by Irving Hartley, including the explosion of the Hindenburg zeppelin in 1937. He and Elda also produced a series of Pan Am travelogues, a prototype for travel shows on television today.

Elda worked as North Carolina State’s Director of Visual Education and in the 1930s she helped found the Documentary Film Association, which exhibited at the first New York World's Fair.

Elda's vision of the future foundation began in 1965, while on a vacation tour to Japan with Alan Watts. She decided to make a film on Zen, and Alan Watts volunteered to narrate the film. At the age of fifty-six, Elda Hartley was on her way to producing documentary films on world religions and spirituality – the beginning of her third career.  

She completed films about the world's spiritual and religious traditions, including documentaries such as Requiem for a Faith and The Sufi Way, and collaborated with Margaret Mead, Joseph Campbell, Edgar Mitchell, Jean Houston, Ram Dass, Alan Watts, Huston Smith and Larry Dossey, among others.

Hartley often said that the goal of her work was greater than simply making films. She died on her 90th birthday, March 6, 2001. After her death, Hartley Films returned the video masters and copyrights to the Smith Family, who allowed them to be viewed on the official Huston Smith websites.

Elder Hartley Films https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLfvx-cbEOmxAByIDUroF0Ie91XO-an0tL

Bombay Ananda Bhavan Hotel, Bangalore https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=yclHBEqndbk
This video is derived from the article 'The Hotels They Stayed In: The Bombay Ananda Bhavan Hotel' which can be found here - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=yclHBEqndbk

Philippe Lavastine visits Sathya Sai Baba in 1960 - he talks about Gurdjieff and the power of the Gurus https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7V609z88H94

The Way To Baba: A Pilgrimage to Prasanthi Nilayam by Elda Hartley https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gew7DsGBrbw

God lives in India https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=CFZoK7dKqQo

Sai Baba : God on Earth https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DuXsOJEaGQY

1990 Swiss documentary 'Who is Sai Baba?' by Victor J. Tognola https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2FhqPZb-3V8&ab_channel=AshramsofIndia

The experiences with Sathya Sai Baba are not always pleasant for the recipient. All genuine gurus do the unexpected, to shock and transform people: Neem Kaloli Baba, Nityananda… Even the ‘gentle’ sage of Arunachala, Ramana Maharshi, set up ashram builder Annamalai Swami to be in conflict with the ashram administrative head and told him not to say that he, Ramana, had told him to go ahead with a building project against the official administration’s plan.

episodes.title: Dr Jack Hislop speaks at the 1989 Sathya Sai Baba Missouri Convention
episodes.description:
Dr Jack Hislop speaks at the Sathya Sai Baba Missouri Convention, May 27th, 1989. The same talk in higher quality vid and with Italian subtitles https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Dw6gujh9HY0&ab_channel=CLAUDIOSAI

A taxi ride in India https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=0eyyz3ORS8w&ab_channel=AshramsofIndia

The subject of abortion https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=6Qru78GHmD4&ab_channel=AshramsofIndia

Karma https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2yrBwVWvGLw&ab_channel=AshramsofIndia

Early Western devotees of Sathya Sai Baba from the 1930s to the 1960s https://ashramsofindia.com/early-western-devotees-of-sathya-sai-baba-from-the-1940s-to-the-1960s/

Early Western devotees of Sathya Sai Baba from the 1970s to the 1980s https://ashramsofindia.com/early-western-devotees-of-sathya-sai-baba-from-the-1970s-to-the-1980s/

Sathya Sai Baba’s Advaita Teachings By John (Jack) Hislop Ph.D.

The following narrative is by Ravi S. Iyer. An edited transcript is available at: http://ravisiyer.blogspot.com/2013/11/sathya-sai-babas-advaita-teachings-by.html

This talk, a significant part of which covers Advaita as taught by Bhagawan Sri Sathya Sai Baba (Swami), was given by Dr John Hislop, in simple and friendly English, at a US Retreat (Eureka, Missouri) on 27th May 1989. The video’s original source is saicast.org and is available at the following saicast links: http://www.saicast.org/1989/19890527Hislop.html and http://vimeo.com/47243347.

About Dr. John Hislop: The late Dr John (Jack) S. Hislop had a Ph.D. (in education) from the University of California, Los Angeles campus (UCLA). [Source: “A PERFECT DISCIPLE, The story of Dr John S Hislop” http://media.radiosai.org/journals/Vol_02/13July01/02_Cover_Story/cover_story.htm]

Dr Hislop first saw Sathya Sai Baba in January 1968. He was a major force in the International Sai Organisation in the 1980s and 1990s. He has written books about his experiences and conversations with Sai Baba, “My Baba and I” “Conversations with Bhagawan Sri Sathya Sai Baba” “Seeking Divinity”

I have given some sub-headings to various sections of the talk along with associated starting time, as follows:

0:00:00 - Prayer-Song
0:01:18 - Introduction by Dr. William (Bill) Harvey
0:06:20 - John (Jack) Hislop begins his talk
0:10:09 - Sathya Sai Baba's Atma Vidya teachings & experiences of Hislop
0:22:53 - How to love Swami?; Hislop experiencing Sathya Sai Baba within
0:33:10 - Advaita; Sathya Sai Baba explaining his (paranormal) knowing of Hislop’s lectures
0:40:59 - Sathya Sai Baba on liberated people in Today’s Age -- Now
0:43:05 - Sathya Sai Baba's Advaita Teachings
1:01:43 - Advaita summarised; practical application of Advaita
1:07:13 - Q&A session; how do you know when you are doing it right?
1:07:51 - Isn’t the process of Inquiry Itself an Illusion?
1:08:33 - How to differentiate between conscience and mental chat?
1:09:59 - Following the I to the Source becomes a burden. How to rid oneself from this burden?
1:12:32 - If we think ourselves as God, won’t we have a big ego?
1:13:51 - Why does Sathya Sai Baba materialise objects and vibhuti before the public?
1:16:14 - Where do human values fit into the context of I-ness being the only genuine thing?
1:20:27 - Sathya Sai Baba saving Hislop and others from a head-on collision on the way from Whitefield to Bangalore A taxi ride in India https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=0eyyz3ORS8w&ab_channel=AshramsofIndia
1:23:39 - Do we need God’s grace for liberation?

Their stories and the places that they visited, many of which are still in existence, are gathered together here in 'Ashrams of India' with practical advice including contact details, directions, and coordinates that link to Google Maps.
